tower.name: Great Plateau Tower
tower.location: Center of the Great Plateau, just southeast of the Temple of Time. It's the first tower in the game and the one the Old Man points you to.
tower.climbTip: No thorns or Malice here, so it's a pure stamina climb. Rest on the angled struts and ledges to refill your wheel, then paraglide off the top to spot the four shrines.

shrines.name: Oman Au Shrine
shrines.location: Northeast of the Great Plateau Tower, on the open plateau; the first shrine the Old Man sends you to.
shrines.oneLine: Grants the Magnesis Trial. Use Magnesis to move the metal blocks and bridge the gaps to reach the monk.

shrines.name: Ja Baij Shrine
shrines.location: East side of the plateau, near the East Abbey ruins.
shrines.oneLine: Grants the Bomb Trial (Remote Bombs). Use round and cube bombs to blast cracked walls and clear a path to the monk.

shrines.name: Owa Daim Shrine
shrines.location: Southeast of Mount Hylia's peak, atop a ledge just outside the mountain's freezing zone (no cold gear needed here).
shrines.oneLine: Grants the Stasis Trial. Freeze the moving platform with Stasis, hit it to load momentum, then ride it across the gap.

shrines.name: Keh Namut Shrine
shrines.location: Northwest of Mount Hylia's peak, in the snowy freezing zone. Bring cold protection (warm food or the Warm Doublet) before heading up.
shrines.oneLine: Grants the Cryonis Trial. Raise ice pillars from the water with Cryonis to make platforms and climb up to the monk.

sideQuests.name: The Old Man's Diary (Warm Doublet)
sideQuests.giver: The Old Man, via his diary in the Woodcutter's House near the Forest of Spirits
sideQuests.oneLine: Read his diary, then cook the dish he forgot: Spicy Meat and Seafood Fry (Raw Meat + Spicy Pepper + Hyrule Bass), and he'll reward you.

notes: Shrine count is exactly four: Oman Au (Magnesis), Ja Baij (Bombs), Owa Daim (Stasis), Keh Namut (Cryonis). All are rune-granting tutorial puzzle shrines: none are combat (no Guardian/Test of Strength), blessing, or quest-gated. Each gives a Spirit Orb; clearing all four is what the Old Man requires before trading you the Paraglider to leave the Plateau. The Owa Daim location was corrected: it sits southeast of Mount Hylia's peak on a ledge OUTSIDE the freezing zone, so it does NOT need cold gear; only Keh Namut and the upper northwest slopes are cold-hazard areas. No Great Fairy Fountain exists on the Great Plateau (greatFairy is correctly null). Note: the draft's fairy-region mapping was wrong and is fixed here. The four BotW Great Fairies are Cotera (near Kakariko Village, Necluda), Kaysa (Tabantha), Mija (Akkala, near Tarrey Town / Dah Hesho), and Tera (Gerudo Desert). The draft swapped Kaysa and Mija. Cotera near Kakariko is the closest fountain to the Plateau. On side quests: the Plateau has no formal 'Side Quests' adventure-log entries; the Warm Doublet task is technically part of the Old Man's main-quest sequence but is the region's one notable optional reward, so it's listed as the de-facto side quest. If you skip it, the Warm Doublet also appears in a chest at the Woodcutter's House after all four shrines are cleared. Confidence high on shrines, tower, and Great Fairy facts; slightly lower on side-quest classification given the main-quest overlap.
changes: Owa Daim Shrine location fixed: it is southeast of Mount Hylia's peak on a ledge OUTSIDE the freezing zone, not on 'Mount Hylia's southern slope' in a cold area. Removed the implication that cold gear is needed for it.
Notes hazard section corrected accordingly: only Keh Namut / upper northwest slopes are the cold-hazard area; Owa Daim is not.

Great Fairy notes corrected: draft swapped Kaysa and Mija. Correct mapping is Cotera (Kakariko/Necluda), Kaysa (Tabantha), Mija (Akkala, near Tarrey Town/Dah Hesho), Tera (Gerudo Desert). Draft had 'Tabantha (Mija)' and 'Tarrey Town (Kaysa)' reversed.
Standardized hint wording to the in-game trial names (Magnesis Trial, Bomb Trial, Stasis Trial, Cryonis Trial) for accuracy.
Refined Keh Namut location from 'high up on snowy Mount Hylia' to 'northwest of Mount Hylia's peak in the snowy freezing zone' for geographic precision.
Added to notes that the Warm Doublet also appears in a chest at the Woodcutter's House after clearing all four shrines (alternate acquisition).
Minor tower climbTip wording tweak: paraglide off the top to 'spot' the four shrines (you don't reach all four directly from the tower).

tower.name: Dueling Peaks Tower
tower.location: On the west bank of the Squabble River in West Necluda, just north of Dueling Peaks Stable, at the foot of the twin peaks.
tower.climbTip: Climb the rock ledges at the base, then scale the lattice, resting on the horizontal beams to recover stamina. No Guardians here, so take a calm, staged climb; eat a stamina meal if you're low.

shrines.name: Ha Dahamar Shrine
shrines.location: In the small pond just west of Dueling Peaks Stable, on the riverbank.
shrines.oneLine: The Water Guides: use Cryonis to clear the entrance spikes, then raise ice on the waterfall wall to steer the orb, pinball-style, into its slot.

shrines.name: Bosh Kala Shrine
shrines.location: West of the Dueling Peaks Tower, just south of the East Post Ruins and west of Proxim Bridge, along the path in from the Great Plateau.
shrines.oneLine: The Wind Guides You: open your paraglider in the updrafts from the fans to float across the gaps and reach the chest and altar.

shrines.name: Ree Dahee Shrine
shrines.location: In the valley between the twin peaks, just north of the Squabble River, between Dueling Peaks Tower and Dueling Peaks Stable.
shrines.oneLine: Timing Is Critical: hit switches to tilt platforms and roll orbs into sockets, activating moving platforms; time your dashes across them.

shrines.name: Toto Sah Shrine
shrines.location: South of Dueling Peaks Stable across the twin bridges, behind a bombable wall along the river near Hickaly Woods.
shrines.oneLine: Toto Sah Apparatus: use the Sheikah Slate to rotate the motion-control platforms into pathways and reach the monk's pedestal.

shrines.name: Ta'loh Naeg Shrine
shrines.location: On the hill directly north of Kakariko Village, up the steps; it's the village's main travel gate.
shrines.oneLine: Ta'loh Naeg's Teaching: a combat tutorial — practice perfect dodge (flurry rush), perfect guard, and the charged spin attack against the Guardian Scout.

shrines.name: Lakna Rokee Shrine
shrines.location: East of Kakariko Village, revealed at the end of the Stolen Heirloom investigation.
shrines.oneLine: Lakna Rokee's Blessing: recover Paya's stolen heirloom, then claim the orb. It's a free blessing once the quest reveals the shrine.

shrines.name: Shee Vaneer Shrine
shrines.location: At the summit of the south twin peak, southeast of Dueling Peaks Tower.
shrines.oneLine: Twin Memories: a mirrored orb puzzle — memorize the orb layout in its twin (Shee Venath) and replicate it here on the 5x5 grid.

shrines.name: Shee Venath Shrine
shrines.location: At the summit of the north twin peak, southeast of Dueling Peaks Tower.
shrines.oneLine: Twin Memories: a mirrored orb puzzle — memorize the orb layout in its twin (Shee Vaneer) and replicate it here on the 5x5 grid.

shrines.name: Hila Rao Shrine
shrines.location: On the Floret Sandbar, an islet where the Hylia River meets Nabi Lake, northeast of Dueling Peaks Tower in West Necluda.
shrines.oneLine: Drifting: first cross Magda's flower field without trampling a bloom to reveal the shrine; inside, use Cryonis on the flowing water.

sideQuests.name: The Stolen Heirloom
sideQuests.oneLine: Investigate the theft of the Sheikah heirloom from Impa's house, tail Dorian at night, and recover the orb — this reveals Lakna Rokee Shrine.

sideQuests.name: Watch Out for the Flowers
sideQuests.oneLine: Cross Magda's flower field to the shrine without stepping on a single bloom, or she turns hostile — this reveals Hila Rao Shrine.

sideQuests.name: Find the Fairy Fountain
sideQuests.oneLine: Help the traveling painter find the nearby Great Fairy Fountain northeast of Kakariko to awaken Cotera; afterward he gives shrine-photo hints.
sideQuests.reward: Reveals Cotera's location; Pikango then offers shrine location hints

sideQuests.name: By Firefly's Light
sideQuests.oneLine: Catch and deliver 5 Sunset Fireflies to Lasli, who misses chasing them at night.

sideQuests.name: Cucco Conundrum
sideQuests.oneLine: Round up Cado's escaped Cuccos and return all 10 to the pen near the chief's house.

greatFairy.name: Great Fairy Cotera
greatFairy.location: In the woods northeast of Kakariko Village (behind/up the path past Ta'loh Naeg Shrine), inside a giant flower bud.
greatFairy.unlockCost: 100 rupees to open the bud the first time. Talk to Pikango first ('Find the Fairy Fountain') to learn her location.

notes: Region has 9 shrines per Game8's Dueling Peaks Tower region page (Hila Rao on Floret Sandbar is filed here, though some sources place it in Central Hyrule — borderline, kept). High confidence on all 9 names and trial subtitles. BIGGEST DRAFT ERROR: the twin-peaks shrines are subtitled 'Twin Memories' (a mirrored 5x5 orb-grid memory puzzle), NOT 'The Two Rings' — there is no arrow-through-rings puzzle and no shrine quest gating them; the draft invented that entirely. They are puzzle shrines, not blessings, and Vaneer=south peak / Venath=north peak. Ha Dahamar's trial is 'The Water Guides' (not 'A Test of Wood') and it sits WEST of Dueling Peaks Stable, not east. Bosh Kala is WEST of the tower (south of East Post Ruins, west of Proxim Bridge), not on the tower's south side. Ree Dahee sits in the valley between the peaks north of the river. Ta'loh Naeg is on the hill north of Kakariko and is a combat tutorial vs a Guardian Scout (correct category). Hila Rao's trial is 'Drifting'. Removed the invented side quest 'Flown the Coop'/'Trissa'/'Curiy Shop' — that is not a real BotW quest (Flown the Coop is a Tears of the Kingdom quest). Cotera is one of only 4 Great Fairies and is correctly assigned to this region; 100-rupee cost confirmed.
changes: Twin peaks (Shee Vaneer/Shee Venath): changed category from 'quest'/blessing to 'puzzle' and replaced the invented 'The Two Rings'/'shoot an arrow through stone rings' hint with the real trial 'Twin Memories' (mirrored 5x5 orb-grid memory puzzle).
Removed the fabricated shrineQuest 'The Two Rings' from both twin-peaks shrines — they are not quest-revealed and that quest does not exist.
Ha Dahamar: fixed trial name from 'A Test of Wood' to 'The Water Guides' and corrected location from 'east of the stable' to the pond WEST of Dueling Peaks Stable; tightened the Cryonis hint.
Bosh Kala: corrected location from 'south side of Dueling Peaks Tower near the river' to west of the tower (south of East Post Ruins, west of Proxim Bridge).
Ree Dahee: corrected location to the valley between the peaks, north of the Squabble River (draft put it east of the stable on the river's east side); used the real trial name 'Timing Is Critical'.
Ta'loh Naeg: corrected location from 'eastern terrace near the Goddess Statue' to the hill directly north of Kakariko; noted it fights a Guardian Scout and is the village travel gate.
Toto Sah & Lakna Rokee & Hila Rao: tightened hints/locations; added Hila Rao's real trial name 'Drifting' and Lakna Rokee's subtitle 'Lakna Rokee's Blessing'.
Removed invented side quest 'Flown the Coop' (giver 'Trissa', 'The Curiy Shop') — not a real BotW quest (it's a Tears of the Kingdom quest).
Added 'Watch Out for the Flowers' (Magda) as an explicit shrine-quest entry in sideQuests.
Fixed 'The Stolen Heirloom' giver attribution to Paya/Dorian (the investigation centers on Paya's theft and tailing Dorian).
Tower: trimmed location wording for accuracy and kept the staged-climb tip.

tower.name: Hateno Tower
tower.location: On a hill in central East Necluda, just northwest of Hateno Village and the road toward Fort Hateno.
tower.climbTip: Base is wrapped in burnable thorny vines and guarded by Bokoblins/Moblins. Burn the thorns (fire arrow or a flame) and climb fast, since they regrow within minutes.

shrines.name: Myahm Agana Shrine
shrines.location: On the hill at the east edge of Hateno Village, near the plot where you can buy a house.
shrines.oneLine: Tilt-maze (Myahm Agana Apparatus): use motion controls to guide the ball, or flip the maze fully upside-down so the ball rolls across the flat back to the goal.

shrines.name: Tahno O'ah Shrine
shrines.location: On the East Necluda coast, near the shore northeast of the Hateno Ancient Tech Lab.
shrines.oneLine: Tahno O'ah's Blessing: no puzzle. Reach the out-of-the-way ledge by the cliffs, enter, and collect the free Spirit Orb from the monk.

shrines.name: Jitan Sa'mi Shrine
shrines.location: At the summit of Mount Lanayru, behind the Goddess Statue at the Spring of Wisdom.
shrines.oneLine: Cold climb. Free corrupted Naydra by shooting all its glowing points, then offer a Naydra's Scale at the Goddess Statue to open the shrine (free orb inside).

shrines.name: Kam Urog Shrine
shrines.location: Northeast of Fort Hateno, in a clearing of statues near a small graveyard south of the Lanayru Promenade.
shrines.oneLine: Talk to Calip and call him 'doctor'. At night a statue's eyes glow purple by the headstones; shoot it with an arrow to raise the buried shrine.

shrines.name: Mezza Lo Shrine
shrines.location: Northeastern East Necluda on Rabia Plain, near Kass's platform southeast of Lanayru Tower (across the Rutala River).
shrines.oneLine: The Crowned Beast: hear Kass's verse, then tame and mount a Mountain Buck (stag) and ride it onto the nearby platform to raise the buried shrine.

shrines.name: Chaas Qeta Shrine
shrines.location: Out in the Necluda Sea on tiny Tenoko Island, southeast of Hateno Village (visible from the coast).
shrines.oneLine: A Major Test of Strength: raft or paraglide out to the island and beat the upgraded Guardian Scout for the orb and ancient gear.

shrines.name: Dow Na'eh Shrine
shrines.location: Along the Lanayru Promenade at the west edge of East Necluda, hidden behind a waterfall.
shrines.oneLine: Three Boxes: find the hidden treasure boxes and set them on the matching pressure switches to open the gate to the monk.

sideQuests.name: The Statue's Bargain
sideQuests.giver: Teebo (boy in Hateno Village; Horned Statue on the village outskirts)
sideQuests.oneLine: Pray to the Horned Statue to sell back a Heart Container or Stamina Vessel for 100 rupees, then rebuy for 120 to re-spec hearts vs stamina.
sideQuests.reward: Ability to swap hearts/stamina (net 20-rupee fee per swap)

sideQuests.name: The Sheep Rustlers
sideQuests.oneLine: Clear the Bokoblins menacing the herd, then drive the three loose sheep back into the pen at Hateno Pasture before time runs out.

sideQuests.name: The Weapon Connoisseur
sideQuests.oneLine: Long quest: show Nebb 8 specific named weapons (Soldier's Broadsword, Knight's gear, etc.) one at a time over many visits.
sideQuests.reward: Rupees per weapon, plus a Diamond on completion

sideQuests.name: A Gift for My Beloved
sideQuests.giver: Manny (boy near the inn in Hateno Village)
sideQuests.oneLine: Find out innkeeper Prima's favorite drink for the lovestruck Manny, then bring her the right gift to win her over.

sideQuests.name: Slated for Upgrades
sideQuests.oneLine: After the lab is powered (Locked Mementos), bring Purah ancient materials and she powers up your Sheikah Slate runes (sensor, camera, etc.).

sideQuests.name: Robbie's Research
sideQuests.giver: Purah (Hateno Ancient Tech Lab; sends you to Robbie at Akkala)
sideQuests.oneLine: Purah sends you to find Robbie at the Akkala Ancient Tech Lab; show him your scars to prove who you are and unlock ancient-tech crafting.
sideQuests.reward: 3 Ancient Arrows and access to Akkala Tech Lab research

notes: Region = Hateno Tower region / East Necluda; the 7-shrine roster is verified and complete (Game8, Gamer Guides, GameFAQs agree): Myahm Agana, Tahno O'ah, Jitan Sa'mi, Kam Urog, Mezza Lo, Chaas Qeta, Dow Na'eh. THREE are quest-gated, not two: Jitan Sa'mi (The Spring of Wisdom), Kam Urog (The Cursed Statue), and Mezza Lo (The Crowned Beast). The draft wrongly described Mezza Lo as a Stasis 'Ancient Trifecta' puzzle revealed without a quest — that conflated it with a different shrine (Mijah Rokeh / the Ancient Trifecta quest). Mezza Lo is buried and raised by riding a tamed Mountain Buck onto Kass's platform on Rabia Plain. Kam Urog's internal monk trial is 'Trial of Passing' (sources also spell it 'Trial of Passage'); minor and not used in a data field. Hateno Tower's base is burnable THORNS guarded by Bokoblins/Moblins, not Malice — the draft's 'Malice with a Guardian-like eye' was invented. Great Fairy: none in this region; greatFairy is null. The nearest is Cotera, whose fountain is northeast of Kakariko Village in West Necluda (a different region) — correct to leave null. Only 4 Great Fairies exist total (Cotera, Kaysa, Mija, Tera). Hazards: Mt. Lanayru summit is freezing — bring cold-resist food/elixirs or warm doublet/Snowquill before the Spring of Wisdom climb; Chaas Qeta needs an offshore trip to Tenoko Island. Side-quest reward fixes: The Sheep Rustlers gives Fresh Milk (not a Ruby), The Weapon Connoisseur ends with a Diamond, and the blue-flame furnace task belongs to the main quest 'Locked Mementos', not 'Slated for Upgrades'. Slightly lower confidence on exact Tech-Lab quest internals than on the shrine roster, which is solid.
changes: Hateno Tower climbTip: removed invented 'thorny Malice with a Guardian-like eye'; the base is ordinary burnable thorny vines guarded by Bokoblins/Moblins, and they regrow within minutes so you must climb fast.

Mezza Lo Shrine: corrected category from 'puzzle' to 'quest' and set shrineQuest to 'The Crowned Beast'. Replaced the invented 'Ancient Trifecta / Stasis boulder' hint (that belongs to a different shrine, Mijah Rokeh) with the real reveal: hear Kass's verse, tame and ride a Mountain Buck onto the platform to raise the buried shrine.
Notes: corrected 'two quest-gated shrines' to THREE (added Mezza Lo / The Crowned Beast alongside Jitan Sa'mi and Kam Urog).
Jitan Sa'mi Shrine: clarified you shoot ALL of Naydra's glowing points (not a single eye) and then offer a Naydra's Scale at the Goddess Statue at the Spring of Wisdom to open the shrine.

The Sheep Rustlers: corrected reward from 'Rupees (and a Ruby in the camp chest)' to the actual quest reward of 10 bottles of Fresh Milk; reworded objective to driving the loose sheep back to the Hateno Pasture pen.
The Weapon Connoisseur: corrected reward to note the final prize is a Diamond (plus rupees per weapon) and specified 8 named weapons.
Slated for Upgrades: removed the inaccurate blue-flame/furnace step (that belongs to the main quest 'Locked Mementos'); the rune upgrades come from bringing Purah ancient materials.
Robbie's Research: clarified the reward (3 Ancient Arrows and access to Akkala Tech Lab ancient-tech research) and the 'show your scars' step.
Minor location wording cleanups (Tahno O'ah, Kam Urog, Dow Na'eh, Mezza Lo) and added that Myahm Agana's mechanism is the Myahm Agana Apparatus.
Verified greatFairy stays null: confirmed Cotera's fountain is in West Necluda near Kakariko, outside this region; only 4 Great Fairies exist total.

tower.name: Lanayru Tower
tower.location: Rises out of the Lanayru Wetlands, north of Kakariko Village and east of the Dueling Peaks, on the road toward Zora's Domain
tower.climbTip: The base sits in a nest of Lizalfos and Wizzrobes. Easiest approach: climb the ladder to the lookout platform northwest of the tower and paraglide across to a high face, skipping most enemies. Lizalfos sleep at night, so attempt it after dark. Rain makes the stone slippery, so bring climbing-boost food or rest on ledges.

shrines.name: Kaya Wan Shrine
shrines.location: Lanayru Wetlands, directly beside the west side of the Wetland Stable, northwest of Lanayru Tower
shrines.oneLine: Shields From Water: use Cryonis to raise ice pillars across the water-filled chambers, navigating past a Guardian Scout to reach the monk.

shrines.name: Daka Tuss Shrine
shrines.location: On a small island in the Lanayru Wetlands, southwest of Lanayru Tower and north of Kakariko Village
shrines.oneLine: Sunken Scoop: use Magnesis on the sunken metal bowl to scoop floating balls and drop them into the cages, then sink the bowl onto the switch.

shrines.name: Sheh Rata Shrine
shrines.location: On a small island in the Hylia River within the Lanayru Wetlands, reachable via an underwater path or raft
shrines.oneLine: Speed of Light: spin the crank to rotate the laser onto the crystal switch, using Cryonis ice pillars to cross the water and reach platforms.

shrines.name: Soh Kofi Shrine
shrines.location: On a cliff just north of Lanayru Tower, near the path to Sidon's bridge; glide over from the tower top
shrines.oneLine: A Minor Test of Strength: defeat the Guardian Scout II with sword and shield, dodging its spin and beam attacks; chest holds a Knight's Bow.

shrines.name: Ne'ez Yohma Shrine
shrines.location: In the center of Zora's Domain, below King Dorephan's throne room
shrines.oneLine: Pushing Power: use Cryonis to shove the Ancient Orb across the sloped waterway into its slot while dodging the boulders rolling down.

shrines.name: Dagah Keek Shrine
shrines.location: Lanayru Great Spring, atop Veiled Falls near Zora's Domain (revealed by the shrine quest)
shrines.oneLine: Blessing shrine revealed by the shrine quest; a free Spirit Orb and a chest with a Silver Rupee wait inside, no puzzle.

shrines.name: Rucco Maag Shrine
shrines.location: On the Samasa Plain in the Lanayru Great Spring, south of Zora's Domain, set within a stone-spike maze patrolled by Lizalfos
shrines.oneLine: Five Flames: light all five torches on the floating cube and pillars with a torch, avoiding the water spout and keeping the flame above water.

shrines.name: Kah Mael Shrine
shrines.location: On Tingel Island, the northernmost island of the chain in the Lanayru Sea (far northeast), hidden under a stone slab
shrines.oneLine: Drop and Rise: lift the slab with an Octo Balloon or Stasis to enter, then shoot the ropes so the cube drops on the scale and launches the barrel up.

shrines.name: Shai Yota Shrine
shrines.location: On a small island in Horon Lagoon in the Lanayru Sea, far east of the region (revealed by the shrine quest)
shrines.oneLine: Free Spirit Orb: the monk grants the orb simply for reaching the shrine; the real challenge is the Master of the Wind quest outside.

sideQuests.name: Frog Catching
sideQuests.giver: Tumbo (Zora child near the inn and general store, Zora's Domain)
sideQuests.oneLine: Bring Tumbo five Hot-Footed Frogs caught around the Domain's pools and waterfalls.

sideQuests.name: Luminous Stone Gathering
sideQuests.giver: Ledo (Zora at the entrance just past the bridge into Zora's Domain)
sideQuests.reward: Diamonds (2 for the first 10 stones, then 1 per 10)
sideQuests.oneLine: Mine Luminous Stones from nearby cliffs and hand them to Ledo in batches of ten.

sideQuests.name: Lynel Safari
sideQuests.giver: Laflat (by the bridge at the eastern end of Zora's Domain)
sideQuests.oneLine: Sneak a photo of the Lynel roaming Ploymus Mountain with the camera rune and bring it back to Laflat.

sideQuests.name: Diving is Beauty!
sideQuests.giver: Gruve (Zora on an upper level by a waterfall in Zora's Domain)
sideQuests.oneLine: Perform a beautiful dive off the high platform into the water below, then report to Gruve.

sideQuests.name: Zora Stone Monuments
sideQuests.giver: Jiahto (Zora historian on the floor below the throne room, Zora's Domain)
sideQuests.oneLine: Read all ten Zora Stone Monuments scattered around Zora's Domain and Vah Ruta's region, then report back to Jiahto.

notes: Verified against Game8, GameFAQs, Zelda Dungeon, and Zeldapedia/Fandom. The Lanayru Tower map sector contains exactly 9 shrines: Kaya Wan, Daka Tuss, Sheh Rata, Soh Kofi, Ne'ez Yohma, Dagah Keek, Rucco Maag, Kah Mael, and Shai Yota. Roster names and count are well sourced. TWO shrines are quest-gated: Dagah Keek (via The Ceremonial Song) and Shai Yota (via Master of the Wind); both are free-orb blessing-style shrines, not internal puzzles, so they are categorized as 'quest'. The Ceremonial Song: talk to Laruta by the fountain, then to Trello on the northwest bridge; recover the Ceremonial Trident from the water with Cryonis + Magnesis, then dive-stab the podium atop Veiled Falls in Zora Armor to reveal Dagah Keek. IMPORTANT GEOGRAPHY FIX: NONE of the 9 Lanayru shrines are on snowy Mount Lanayru. Kah Mael (Tingel Island) and Shai Yota (Horon Lagoon) are both on low, warm islands in the Lanayru Sea, NOT on Mount Lanayru, so no cold-resistance gear is needed for them. Hazards: the Wetlands have constant rain that makes climbing slippery, so bring climbing-boost food or a stamina cushion; the Lanayru Tower area is heavily infested with Lizalfos and Wizzrobes (clear at night). Zora's Domain itself and the waterfalls around it are far easier to traverse with the Zora Armor (lets you swim up waterfalls), which you receive early in the Vah Ruta storyline. The Sea-island shrines (Kah Mael, Shai Yota) and Rucco Maag are reached on foot/glider, not by deep diving. No Great Fairy Fountain exists in Lanayru; the four fountains are Cotera (West Necluda), Kaysa (Tabantha/Ridgeland), Mija (Akkala), and Tera (Gerudo) — greatFairy is null. The 'Find the Fairy Fountain' quest points to Cotera in Necluda, not Lanayru. Lower confidence remains on exact rupee amounts for Lynel Safari (varies by report) and minor flavor wording.
changes: Daka Tuss hint was wrong: the puzzle is a Magnesis 'scoop' with a sunken metal bowl to lift floating balls into cages, not dropping a metal ball onto a seesaw. Rewrote the oneLine.

Sheh Rata location refined to 'small island in the Hylia River, Lanayru Wetlands' (draft said vaguely 'northern Wetlands, west of Zora's Domain'); hint corrected to the rotating-laser + crystal-switch + Cryonis-pillar puzzle (draft's 'raise water and float the orb' was inaccurate).

Ne'ez Yohma hint corrected: the orb is moved with Cryonis up a sloped waterway while dodging boulders, not 'water current and Stasis-timed pushes'. Location refined to 'below King Dorephan's throne room'.

Rucco Maag location corrected: it is on the Samasa Plain in the Lanayru Great Spring (south of Zora's Domain) inside a stone-spike maze, NOT 'Lanayru Sea coast, partly underwater'. Removed the 'partly underwater' and 'Vah Ruta's perch' errors.
Kaya Wan hint was completely wrong: the trial uses Cryonis ice pillars to cross water and pass a Guardian Scout, not Magnesis metal crates blocking flame jets and water spouts. Rewrote the oneLine.

Kah Mael location was WRONG: it is on Tingel Island, the northernmost island in the Lanayru Sea (hidden under a stone slab), NOT on Mount Lanayru's snowy western slopes. Hint corrected to the scale puzzle: lift the slab (Octo Balloon/Stasis), then shoot the ropes so the cube drops and launches the barrel.

Shai Yota location was WRONG: it is on an island in Horon Lagoon in the Lanayru Sea (far east), NOT on Mount Lanayru near Naydra/Spring of Wisdom. It is also quest-gated, not a wind puzzle: changed category from 'puzzle' to 'quest', set shrineQuest to 'Master of the Wind', and rewrote the hint (the monk gives the orb for free on entry; 'Master of the Wind' is the external shrine quest, not an internal puzzle).
Zora Stone Monuments reward corrected from 'Sapphire' to 'Diamond' (Jiahto rewards a Diamond for all ten monuments). Added that Jiahto is on the floor below the throne room.
Fixed side-quest giver locations (Tumbo near inn/general store; Ledo at the bridge entrance; Laflat by the eastern bridge; Gruve on an upper level).

Rewrote the notes: corrected the false claim that some Lanayru shrines are on snowy Mount Lanayru and require cold resistance (none are; the two Sea-island shrines are warm/low). Noted there are TWO quest-gated shrines (Dagah Keek and Shai Yota), not one. Fixed The Ceremonial Song details: named Laruta and Trello and corrected the dive-stab location to atop Veiled Falls (draft said 'East Reservoir waterfall').

Tower location/climbTip rewritten: corrected to 'north of Kakariko Village, east of Dueling Peaks', and replaced the vague climb advice with the real route (ladder to the northwest lookout platform, glide across, attempt at night when Lizalfos sleep).
Confidence raised from 0.86 to 0.90 after multi-source verification of the roster, two quest gates, geography fixes, and the corrected Diamond reward.

tower.name: Lake Tower
tower.location: On the small island in the middle of Lake Hylia, just north of the Bridge of Hylia in south-central Hyrule.
tower.climbTip: The tower base sits in open water, so glide in or swim across from the Bridge of Hylia. No Malice or thorns here, but stamina matters: top up stamina food before the climb. From the top you can paraglide west toward Hylia Island to reach Ya Naga.

shrines.name: Ka'o Makagh Shrine
shrines.location: On a high ledge a short climb up the hill just south of Highland Stable, overlooking the stable.
shrines.oneLine: Trial 'Metal Doors Open the Way' — use Magnesis to move the metal doors and use them as ramps/bridges to reach the monk.

shrines.name: Pumaag Nitae Shrine
shrines.location: In the woods southeast of Lake Hylia, near the border of Pagos Woods and Finra Woods, a short trip from Lake Tower.
shrines.oneLine: 'A Minor Test of Strength' — defeat the Guardian Scout II (sword and shield); use Flurry Rush on its melee swings.

shrines.name: Ishto Soh Shrine
shrines.location: Atop a cliff west of Oseira Plains and east of Daval Peak, in the southwest of the Lake region.
shrines.oneLine: Trial 'Bravery's Grasp' — set the portable laser on the moving platform to hit the crystal switch, then climb the raised ledges.

shrines.name: Ya Naga Shrine
shrines.location: In the center of Hylia Island, the large island in Lake Hylia; paraglide in from Lake Tower to the west.
shrines.oneLine: Trial 'Shatter the Heavens' — use Stasis/Remote Bombs to drive the stone cube up its track and smash the blocks to launch up.

shrines.name: Shae Katha Shrine
shrines.location: Behind the Goddess Statue at the Spring of Courage, by Damel Forest north of the Zonai Ruins, southeast of Lake Hylia.
shrines.oneLine: Blessing shrine — no inner puzzle. The door behind the Goddess Statue opens only after the shrine quest.

shrines.name: Shoqa Tatone Shrine
shrines.location: Rises on Puffer Beach on the southern coast, south of Lake Tower, only after its shrine quest is solved.
shrines.oneLine: Hidden until you finish the quest; once it surfaces, place the orb to raise it and walk in for the blessing — no inner puzzle.

sideQuests.name: The Horseback Hoodlums
sideQuests.oneLine: Perosa asks you to drive off the Blue Bokoblins riding horses around the stable; defeat the five mounted Bokoblins on Fural Plain.

sideQuests.name: The Serpent's Jaws
sideQuests.giver: Kass (in the Pagos Woods near the Spring of Courage)
sideQuests.oneLine: Solve Kass's verse: drop a Farosh's Scale into the Spring of Courage to open the door behind the Goddess Statue to Shae Katha.
sideQuests.reward: Access to Shae Katha Shrine (Thunderspear chest + Spirit Orb)

sideQuests.name: Guardian Slideshow
sideQuests.oneLine: Photograph three Guardian types (Scout, Skywatcher, Stalker) for Loone; she gives you her orb 'Roscoe' to raise Shoqa Tatone Shrine.
sideQuests.reward: Access to Shoqa Tatone Shrine (Spirit Orb)

notes: All 6 Lake Tower shrines confirmed: Ka'o Makagh, Pumaag Nitae, Ishto Soh, Ya Naga, Shae Katha, Shoqa Tatone. Two need shrine quests: Shae Katha via 'The Serpent's Jaws' (Kass; drop a Farosh's Scale in the Spring of Courage) and Shoqa Tatone via 'Guardian Slideshow' (Loone, Puffer Beach; photograph Guardian Scout, Skywatcher, and Stalker). Both are blessing-style (no inner puzzle). KEY FIXES: the draft's Ishto Soh trial was wrong — it is 'Bravery's Grasp' (portable laser + Crystal Switch + moving platform), NOT 'The Two Rings' with a ball; its location is southwest (cliff west of Oseira Plains, east of Daval Peak), not 'above Lake Hylia's east side.' Ya Naga's trial name 'Shatter the Heavens' was right but the hint was wrong — it uses a stone cube on a vertical track driven by Stasis/Remote Bombs, not crystal switches/rolling boulders. Ka'o Makagh ('Metal Doors Open the Way', Magnesis) and Pumaag Nitae ('A Minor Test of Strength', Guardian Scout II) trial names are correct; Pumaag's hint was tweaked since the Scout II is a melee sword/shield foe, not a beam attacker. NO Great Fairy Fountain in the Lake region — the four Great Fairies (Cotera near Kakariko, Kaysa in Tabantha, Mija in Akkala, Tera in the Gerudo Desert) are all elsewhere. Lakeside Stable's 'Thunder Magnet' (Cima) belongs to the Faron/Lakeside zone and is correctly left out. Hazards: open water around Lake Tower and Hylia Island (drowning/stamina, no cold here); Lynel-free; expect Guardian Scouts in the combat shrine and Ka'o Makagh — no special armor required. Confidence raised after web-verifying every trial name and location against GameFAQs, Zelda Wiki/Dungeon, Game8, and Zelda Universe.
changes: Ishto Soh: replaced fabricated trial 'The Two Rings' (ball through rotating rings) with the real trial 'Bravery's Grasp' (portable laser + Crystal Switch + moving platform). The draft itself flagged this as unverified recall — it was indeed wrong.
Ishto Soh: corrected the location — it is atop a cliff west of Oseira Plains, east of Daval Peak in the southwest of the Lake region, not 'on high ground above Lake Hylia's east side.'

Ya Naga: rewrote the hint for 'Shatter the Heavens' — the trial uses a stone cube on a vertical track driven up by Stasis/Remote Bombs to smash blocks and launch Link, NOT crystal switches and rolling boulders/levers. Trial name was already correct.
Pumaag Nitae: fixed the combat hint — the Guardian Scout II is a melee sword-and-shield foe, so Flurry Rush its swings (the draft said 'use Flurry Rush on its beam attacks').
Pumaag Nitae: refined the location to the Pagos Woods / Finra Woods border southeast of Lake Hylia (draft's 'east of Lake Tower, north of Highland Stable' was off).
Ka'o Makagh: sharpened the hint — Magnesis is used to move the metal doors and use them as ramps/bridges, not merely 'slide the doors aside.'
The Horseback Hoodlums: specified the targets as five Blue Bokoblins on horseback at Fural Plain (was vague 'Bokoblin riders').

The Serpent's Jaws: noted the reward chest contains a Thunderspear in addition to the Spirit Orb; clarified Kass sings in the Pagos Woods near the Spring of Courage; the scale is a Farosh's Scale dropped into the spring to open the door behind the Goddess Statue.
Guardian Slideshow: named the three required Guardian types (Scout, Skywatcher, Stalker) and Loone's orb 'Roscoe'.
Tower climbTip: trimmed the inaccurate 'Sheikah-tech updraft' suggestion (no convenient updraft at Lake Tower) and kept the paraglide-to-Ya-Naga note.
Raised confidence from 0.82 to 0.93 after web-verifying all trial names, locations, and quest details. Great Fairy assignment (none) confirmed correct.

tower.name: Faron Tower
tower.location: Rises from the Hill of Baltha, in the wooded highlands northeast of Lakeside Stable, overlooking the Faron rainforest and Calora Lake.
tower.climbTip: The tower is wrapped in thorny vines that damage you on contact. Burn them off with a fire arrow or torch, or hop between the bare stone gaps, then climb. Bring stamina food; it is a tall climb in humid heat.

shrines.name: Shai Utoh Shrine
shrines.location: Inside a cave behind a breakable rock wall at the base of Ubota Point, just behind Lakeside Stable.
shrines.oneLine: Halt the Tilt: use Stasis to freeze the tilting platforms and climb up. You can also pin them with the metal chest via Magnesis.

shrines.name: Shoda Sah Shrine
shrines.location: Behind the waterfall flowing from Riola Spring, southeast in the Faron rainforest.
shrines.oneLine: Impeccable Timing: load an orb in the launcher and hit the crystal switch when the moving platforms are farthest apart to land it in the receptacle.

shrines.name: Yah Rin Shrine
shrines.location: On the cliffs on the northwestern outskirts of Lurelin Village, East Necluda, overlooking the village.
shrines.oneLine: A Weighty Decision: stand on one scale and use Magnesis to lift the metal cube high over the other scale, then drop it to launch yourself up.

shrines.name: Muwo Jeem Shrine
shrines.location: Atop Cape Cales, the cape overlooking the Necluda Sea in southeastern East Necluda, reached by gliding or climbing the cliffs.
shrines.oneLine: A Modest Test of Strength: beat the Guardian Scout III. Dodge for flurry rushes and use the breakable stone pillars as cover or to stun it.

shrines.name: Qukah Nata Shrine
shrines.location: Rises from a mound in Calora Lake, atop a waterfall directly east of Faron Tower.
shrines.oneLine: A Song of Storms: equip metal gear and stand on the mound in a thunderstorm so lightning strikes it, revealing the shrine; inside is a free orb.

shrines.name: Kah Yah Shrine
shrines.location: At the Palmorae Ruins, the curving spit of land east of Lurelin Village (a fragment sits out at Soka Point); revealed after restoring the broken monument.
shrines.oneLine: Quick Thinking: carry a barrel onto the moving platform, crouch under the barriers, then set it on the far floor switch to open the gate.

shrines.name: Korgu Chideh Shrine
shrines.location: On Koholit Rock at the summit of Eventide Island, far southeast in the Necluda Sea.
shrines.oneLine: Stranded on Eventide: stripped of gear, carry the three Ancient Orbs to their pedestals to raise the shrine; inside is a free orb.

shrines.name: Tawa Jinn Shrine
shrines.location: Buried on the eastern slope of Mount Taran, above Taran Pass on the Faron/East Necluda border; rises once the quest is done.
shrines.oneLine: The Three Giant Brothers: take the Ancient Orb from each of the three Hinox on Mount Taran and place them in the pedestals to raise the shrine; inside is a free orb.

sideQuests.name: Thunder Magnet
sideQuests.oneLine: Lightning keeps hitting the stable. Climb to the roof and use Magnesis to pull out the metal Woodcutter's Axe lodged in the wooden horse head.

sideQuests.name: Take Back the Sea
sideQuests.oneLine: Monsters have overrun Aris Beach east of the village. Clear out every enemy in the stronghold there, then report back to Sebasto.

sideQuests.name: The Hero's Cache
sideQuests.giver: Kass, on a stone pillar at Kitano Bay (east Faron coast)
sideQuests.oneLine: Solve Kass's riddle '17 of 24': at 5 PM the pillar's shadow points to a chest in the water. Pull it up with Magnesis to claim the cache.

notes: Region grouping follows the standard tower-reveal convention (Game8/Thonky): "Faron" = the map area uncovered by Faron Tower, which is 8 shrines. Faron is tropical, hot and humid, with frequent thunderstorms around Calora Lake - bring the Rubber armor set and unequip metal gear in storms or lightning will hit you. KEY FIXES vs the draft: (1) Tawa Jinn is NOT a free walk-in blessing - the shrine is buried and only rises after completing the shrine quest "The Three Giant Brothers" (collect one Ancient Orb from each of three Hinox on Mount Taran and place them in the three pedestals). Its interior has no trial, just a chest and a free orb, but you cannot reach it without the quest, so it is quest-gated like Qukah Nata and Korgu Chideh. (2) Yah Rin is NOT on an island near Soka Point - it sits on the cliffs on the northwestern outskirts of Lurelin Village in East Necluda. (3) Muwo Jeem's foe is a Guardian Scout III (a strong scout), not a "Minor Guardian Scout." (4) Kah Yah's shrine is at the Palmorae Ruins (a fragment for the quest is found out at Soka Point), not on Soka Point itself. So FOUR of the 8 are quest-gated: Qukah Nata (A Song of Storms, hinted by Kass), Kah Yah (A Fragmented Monument, given by Garini at the Palmorae Ruins), Korgu Chideh (Stranded on Eventide, auto-starts on the island - a tough no-gear survival challenge, recommend a first-timer wait), and Tawa Jinn (The Three Giant Brothers). Geography caveats: Muwo Jeem (Cape Cales), Yah Rin (Lurelin outskirts), Qukah Nata (Calora Lake), and Tawa Jinn (Mount Taran/Taran Pass) sit in or on the East Necluda border but are part of the Faron Tower region. A Fragmented Monument and the three shrine-reveal quests are technically shrine quests in-game rather than pure side quests. No Great Fairy Fountain is in Faron - the four fountains (Cotera, Kaysa, Mija, Tera) are in West Necluda, Tabantha/Rito, Akkala, and Gerudo. Shrine names, monk names, quest names, locations, foes, and rewards cross-checked against Game8, Thonky, GameFAQs, Zelda Dungeon, and Zelda Wiki/Fandom. High confidence on the corrected dataset.
changes: MAJOR: Tawa Jinn Shrine corrected - it is NOT a free walk-in 'blessing' shrine. It is buried and revealed only by completing the shrine quest 'The Three Giant Brothers' (collect an Ancient Orb from each of three Hinox on Mount Taran and place them in the pedestals). Changed category 'blessing'->'quest', set shrineQuest to 'The Three Giant Brothers', and rewrote the oneLine. The interior has no trial (chest + free orb), but it is quest-gated.

MAJOR: Yah Rin Shrine location was wrong. The draft placed it 'on a small island in the Necluda Sea off the Faron coast near Soka Point.' It actually sits on the cliffs on the northwestern outskirts of Lurelin Village in East Necluda, overlooking the village. Corrected the location.

Muwo Jeem hint named the wrong enemy: it is a Guardian Scout III (a strong scout with an Ancient Battle Axe+ and Guardian Shield+), not a 'Minor Guardian Scout.' Also reworded the combat tip to use the breakable stone pillars as cover/stun, which is the real arena mechanic.

Kah Yah Shrine location refined: the shrine is at the Palmorae Ruins (the curving spit east of Lurelin Village), not 'on Soka Point, a small island.' Soka Point is only where the third monument fragment is found. Tightened the interior hint to a single-barrel route.

Notes corrected from 'three of the 8 are quest-gated' to FOUR (added Tawa Jinn), fixed the A Fragmented Monument giver location to 'Garini at the Palmorae Ruins,' removed the now-resolved Yah Rin/Soka Point uncertainty, and noted the Great Fairy regions more precisely (Kaysa is Tabantha/Rito).

Minor wording polish on Shai Utoh (clarified Stasis-then-climb plus the Magnesis-chest pin) and The Hero's Cache (added that you pull the chest up with Magnesis). No factual change. Confirmed correct and unchanged: 8-shrine roster and all shrine/monk names, Qukah Nata location/quest (A Song of Storms), Korgu Chideh/Stranded on Eventide, Shai Utoh, Shoda Sah, all three side-quest rewards (Rubber Helm, Silver Rupee, Gold Rupee), and that Faron has no Great Fairy Fountain.

tower.name: Central Tower
tower.location: On the edge of Hyrule Field just west of Hyrule Castle, in the open expanse near the Castle Town Ruins.
tower.climbTip: Ringed by Decayed Guardians and patrolled by Guardian Stalkers that laser you mid-climb. Approach from the south/west using ruins as cover, climb fast, and clear or distract the Guardians (or wear climbing/Ancient gear) first.

shrines.name: Kaam Ya'tak Shrine
shrines.location: Directly southwest of Central Tower, at the base of Mount Daphnes just west of Windvane Meadow in Hyrule Field.
shrines.oneLine: Trial of Power. Use Magnesis and Stasis to swing the hanging boulder, then time your sprint past the rolling spiked boulders.

shrines.name: Rota Ooh Shrine
shrines.location: At Outskirt Stable, in southwestern Central Hyrule along the road southwest of the Castle Town Ruins.
shrines.oneLine: Passing of the Gates. Use Stasis and timing to slip past the spinning gates, and Magnesis metal blocks to bridge gaps and hit switches.

shrines.name: Wahgo Katta Shrine
shrines.location: Southeast Central Hyrule, right beside Riverside Stable near the Hylia River.
shrines.oneLine: Metal Connections. Use Magnesis to move the metal crates and complete the circuit, building a path to the monk.

shrines.name: Katah Chuki Shrine
shrines.location: Southwest of Hyrule Castle, out in Hyrule Field near the Castle Town Ruins.
shrines.oneLine: A Minor Test of Strength. Beat the Guardian Scout; flurry rush its swings and use the chest weapons to finish it quickly.

shrines.name: Saas Ko'sah Shrine
shrines.location: Inside Hyrule Castle at the Docks, on the northwest side where water leads into the castle. Light the central torch to raise the buried shrine.
shrines.oneLine: A Major Test of Strength. Hardest Guardian Scout IV in the game; bring strong weapons, dodge for flurry rushes, lift the metal floor blocks with Magnesis for cover.

shrines.name: Dah Kaso Shrine
shrines.location: Under the Digdogg Suspension Bridge in southwestern Central Hyrule, near the Regencia River.
shrines.oneLine: A Minor Test of Strength. Defeat the Guardian Scout with flurry rushes and the weapons it drops.

shrines.name: Namika Ozz Shrine
shrines.location: Northeast Central Hyrule in the Crenel Hills, hidden inside a large hollowed tree stump.
shrines.oneLine: A Modest Test of Strength. Fight the stronger Guardian Scout III; flurry rush its spear and axe, then grab the Frostspear from the chest.

shrines.name: Noya Neha Shrine
shrines.location: North of Central Tower (east of Ridgeland Tower) on the island west of Hyrule Castle, in a cave behind thorny vines and a cracked wall.
shrines.oneLine: A Minor Test of Strength. Burn the thorns, bomb the cracked wall to open the cave, then beat the Guardian Scout inside.

sideQuests.name: The Royal White Stallion
sideQuests.oneLine: Tame the pure-white royal horse roaming near Safula Hill and register it at the stable to claim the royal gear.

sideQuests.name: My Hero
sideQuests.giver: Aliza, standing under the trees across the road from Outskirt Stable
sideQuests.oneLine: Show Aliza the Master Sword (you must already have pulled it) to prove you are the hero she's waiting for.

sideQuests.name: A Rare Find
sideQuests.oneLine: Trott is sick of vegetarian meals; bring him fresh Raw Gourmet Meat (from Hebra/Gerudo wildlife) for his reward.

sideQuests.name: A Royal Recipe
sideQuests.oneLine: Cook the royal kitchen's Fruitcake (or Monster Cake) and bring it to Gotter; the recipes are in Hyrule Castle's library.
sideQuests.reward: Silver Rupee (300 rupees); a second cake earns another

sideQuests.name: The Royal Guard's Gear
sideQuests.oneLine: Parcy wants to see Royal Guard equipment; retrieve any Royal Guard armor piece from Hyrule Castle and show it to her.

notes: SHRINE COUNT: 8, matching TheGamer and Game8's Central Tower Region breakdown (Kaam Ya'tak, Rota Ooh, Wahgo Katta, Katah Chuki, Saas Ko'sah, Dah Kaso, Namika Ozz, Noya Neha). NOTE: some sites (e.g. Zelda Dungeon) group "Central Hyrule" more broadly and list ~29 shrines; this app uses the per-tower Central Tower Region convention = exactly these 8. None are gated by a shrine quest, so no category=quest entries. Split: 3 puzzle, 5 combat Tests of Strength. Monk names match shrine names, so none listed separately. GREAT FAIRY: null is correct — all four fountains (Cotera/West Necluda, Kaysa/Ridgeland-Tabantha, Mija/Akkala, Tera/Gerudo) are outside this region. HAZARDS: the most dangerous overworld region — Hyrule Field and the Castle swarm with Guardian Stalkers and Decayed Guardians whose lasers one-shot early players. Recommended: Ancient armor (laser resistance) or strong shield-parry; Saas Ko'sah sits deep in the castle Docks, so most players leave it until well-equipped. CONFIDENCE: shrine roster, categories, tower, and Great Fairy are very high. Side-quest rewards verified via Game8/Fandom: A Rare Find, A Royal Recipe, and The Royal Guard's Gear all give a Silver Rupee/300 rupees (a Silver Rupee = 300 rupees). Minor uncertainty remains only on exact NPC positioning wording.
changes: A Rare Find: FIXED invented content. Draft credited 'Domidak and Prissen' and a luminous-stone wager — both wrong. Real quest is given by Trott at Outskirt Stable, who wants Raw Gourmet Meat; reward is a Silver Rupee. Domidak/Prissen are twins at Dueling Peaks Stable (different region, tied to 'Misko, the Great Bandit').
The Royal Guard's Gear: corrected giver to Parcy at Riverside Stable (draft hedged it as a 'note/diary'). Reward is 300 Rupees for showing her any Royal Guard armor piece from Hyrule Castle.
A Royal Recipe: specified reward as Silver Rupee (300 rupees) and noted Monster Cake also works; rewritten hint to mention the castle-library recipes.

Saas Ko'sah Shrine: corrected location from 'directly north of the castle's heart' to the Docks on the castle's northwest side, and added that you must light the central torch to raise the buried shrine. Hint clarified to Guardian Scout IV and Magnesis floor-block cover.
Namika Ozz Shrine: corrected region placement from 'east of Hyrule Castle' to 'northeast Central Hyrule' (Crenel Hills).
Noya Neha Shrine: tightened location to the island west of Hyrule Castle and clarified the cave entry (burn thorns, bomb cracked wall).
Wahgo Katta hint: corrected — the circuit builds a path/platforms to the monk rather than powering a 'moving walkway'.
My Hero: clarified Aliza stands under the trees across the road from Outskirt Stable.
Tower climbTip: named the specific threats (Decayed Guardians around the tower, patrolling Guardian Stalkers) for accuracy.

Notes: added clarification that the 8-shrine count follows the Central Tower Region convention (vs. broader ~29-shrine 'Central Hyrule' groupings), and noted a Silver Rupee = 300 rupees. Raised confidence to 0.93 after web-verifying all side-quest givers/rewards.

tower.name: Wasteland Tower
tower.location: On a rock platform in the middle of a sinkhole/quicksand basin at the western edge of the Gerudo Desert, west of Gerudo Town.
tower.climbTip: The base sits in sinking sand patrolled by enemies. Land on the central rock pillar or paraglide in from a higher ledge to the east, then make the long vertical climb; bring stamina-restoring food.

shrines.name: Daqo Chisay Shrine
shrines.location: Just outside the front gate of Gerudo Town, on a ledge to the right of the entrance.
shrines.oneLine: The Whole Picture: use Magnesis to slide metal objects into the gaps in the floor wires so current flows from the source to the gates and chest.

shrines.name: Kay Noh Shrine
shrines.location: On a small cliff overlooking Gerudo Canyon Stable, at the east mouth of Gerudo Canyon.
shrines.oneLine: Power of Electricity: use Magnesis to set metal cubes in the water so they bridge the current from the power sources to the switches.

shrines.name: Jee Noh Shrine
shrines.location: On the cliff edge inside Gerudo Canyon Pass, at its southernmost curve, east of the Wasteland Tower.
shrines.oneLine: On the Move: use moving conveyor platforms and Stasis, and shoot the ancient orb to knock it into the receptacle to open each gate.

shrines.name: Hawa Koth Shrine
shrines.location: Beneath the Gerudo Great Skeleton in the far southwest corner of the Gerudo Desert.
shrines.oneLine: The Current Solution: use Magnesis to move metal blocks and conduct the electric current across the gaps to the switches.

shrines.name: Misae Suma Shrine
shrines.location: East Barrens, east of the Southern Oasis in the southern Gerudo Desert (a sleeping Gerudo, Pokki, blocks it).
shrines.oneLine: Free orb once unlocked; complete the shrine quest by bringing Pokki the drink Noble Pursuit to wake her and open the shrine.

shrines.name: Raqa Zunzo Shrine
shrines.location: South of Gerudo Town, by the sand-seal race course.
shrines.oneLine: Win the sand-seal race (beat the target time) to reveal the shrine; inside is a blessing (free orb).

shrines.name: Dako Tah Shrine
shrines.location: On the Great Cliffs north of Kara Kara Bazaar, northeast of Gerudo Town (a sandstorm covers the area by day).
shrines.oneLine: Talk to Nobiro at Kara Kara Bazaar to start the quest; reach the shrine, then solve its electric Magnesis-cube puzzles inside.

shrines.name: Korsh O'hu Shrine
shrines.location: Buried in the sand at the East Gerudo Ruins, southwest of the Wasteland Tower.
shrines.oneLine: Match each symbol-marked metal orb to the Seven Heroine statue bearing the same symbol; placing all of them raises the shrine.

shrines.name: Kema Zoos Shrine
shrines.location: Northwest of the West Gerudo Ruins, out in the desert northwest of Gerudo Town.
shrines.oneLine: Follow the swordswomen statues: at each one, head in the direction its sword points to find the next, leading to the shrine.

shrines.name: Dila Maag Shrine
shrines.location: At the center of the South Lomei Labyrinth, on an island off the south Gerudo Desert coast.
shrines.oneLine: Navigate the maze to its center; the shrine is a blessing, and a chest inside holds the Barbarian Armor (chest piece).

shrines.name: Suma Sahma Shrine
shrines.location: Atop Mount Granajh, southeast of the Wasteland Tower toward the Gerudo Highlands/Faron border.
shrines.oneLine: Read the Mountain Peak Log, then at 4PM cast a cold shadow (snowball or Cryonis block) on the glowing pedestal to raise the shrine.

shrines.name: Tho Kayu Shrine
shrines.location: Buried in the Toruma Dunes, west of Gerudo Town and west of Daqo Chisay Shrine (a Molduga roams the area).
shrines.oneLine: Light the four torches on the surrounding rocks to raise the buried shrine, then claim the free orb (watch for the Molduga).

sideQuests.name: The Search for Barta
sideQuests.oneLine: Find the missing guard Barta, hiding near the Gerudo Great Skeleton, and report back to Liana.

sideQuests.name: Tools of the Trade
sideQuests.reward: Reopens the jewelry shop; counts toward the Thunder Helm
sideQuests.oneLine: Bring Isha 10 Flint so she can get her jewelry stand running again.

sideQuests.name: Medicinal Molduga
sideQuests.reward: Gold Rupee; counts toward the Thunder Helm
sideQuests.oneLine: Bring Malena Molduga Guts to treat her sick husband; fight the Molduga from a rock or ledge with bombs.

sideQuests.name: The Mystery Polluter
sideQuests.oneLine: Follow the trail of Hydromelon rinds to Calyban on the wall by the water, who is polluting Dalia's garden, and get her to stop.

sideQuests.name: The Secret Club's Secret
sideQuests.giver: Begins on trying to enter the Gerudo Secret Club (Greta's shop)
sideQuests.reward: Access to the Gerudo Secret Club (sells Desert Voe and Radiant sets)
sideQuests.oneLine: Eavesdrop at The Noble Canteen to learn the password 'GSC' (plus a diamond) and gain entry. Optional; not needed for the Thunder Helm.

sideQuests.name: The Thunder Helm
sideQuests.oneLine: Complete the four Gerudo Town favors (Barta, Tools of the Trade, Medicinal Molduga, Mystery Polluter), then Riju lends you the Thunder Helm.

greatFairy.name: Tera
greatFairy.location: Inside the rib cage of the Gerudo Great Skeleton, far southwest corner of the Gerudo Desert (beside Hawa Koth Shrine).
greatFairy.unlockCost: 100 / 500 / 1,000 / 10,000 Rupees, depending on how many other Great Fairies you have already awakened.

notes: The Gerudo region maps to the in-game Wasteland Tower region, which has exactly 12 shrines (cross-confirmed by Gamer Guides, Zelda Dungeon, GameFAQs, Game8). Roster (unchanged from the draft, all 12 names are correct): Daqo Chisay, Kay Noh, Jee Noh, Hawa Koth, Misae Suma, Raqa Zunzo, Dako Tah, Korsh O'hu, Kema Zoos, Dila Maag, Suma Sahma, Tho Kayu. Eight are standalone; five are quest-gated; Tho Kayu is a buried blessing revealed by lighting torches. The neighboring Gerudo Tower region (Gerudo Highlands: Keeha Yoog, Joloo Nah, Kema Kosassa, etc.) is a SEPARATE region and is correctly excluded. Great Fairy Tera is correctly placed (one of only four Great Fairies). The biggest draft errors were geography/mechanics, not the roster: Dako Tah and Tho Kayu locations were essentially swapped/misplaced, Jee Noh was mislocated to the Great Skeleton, and several quest hints were inaccurate. Note the Thunder Helm needs only FOUR favors, not five (the Secret Club's Secret is optional). Hazards: extreme heat by day (Desert Voe/Gerudo armor or chilly/cooling food) and freezing cold at night (Warm Doublet or spicy food); Molduga roam the open sand basins (Toruma Dunes, near the Great Skeleton) and surface to attack -- fight from rock platforms. Entry to Gerudo Town requires the vai (women's) disguise from the Forbidden City Entry main quest, bought at Kara Kara Bazaar. Remaining uncertainty: I could not 100% confirm whether Jee Noh is canonically tagged 'Wasteland Tower' vs 'Gerudo Highlands' by every guide (it sits right on the Gerudo Canyon boundary), but the major 12-shrine guides count it in the Wasteland Tower region; confidence on that one item is slightly lower.
changes: Tho Kayu Shrine location corrected: it is buried in the Toruma Dunes west of Gerudo Town (west of Daqo Chisay), NOT in the Great Cliffs near Birida Lookout/Gerudo Tower (that area belongs to the Gerudo Highlands). Its draft location appeared swapped with Dako Tah.

Tho Kayu hint corrected: the shrine is buried and is revealed by lighting four torches on the surrounding rocks (with a Molduga nearby); it then gives a free orb. The draft's 'no puzzle, just reach it' was misleading.

Dako Tah Shrine location corrected: it is on the Great Cliffs north of Kara Kara Bazaar (northeast of Gerudo Town), NOT atop the East Gerudo Ruins/Toruma Dunes. The quest is started by talking to Nobiro at the Kara Kara Bazaar lookout, and a sandstorm covers the area by day. Hint rewritten accordingly.

Jee Noh Shrine location corrected: it is on the cliff edge in Gerudo Canyon Pass at its southernmost curve (east of the Wasteland Tower), NOT 'north of the Gerudo Great Skeleton in the northwest desert.'
Jee Noh hint corrected: 'On the Move' uses moving conveyor platforms plus Stasis and shooting an ancient orb into a receptacle -- it is NOT a tilt-the-tray motion-control maze.

Korsh O'hu hint corrected: 'The Seven Heroines' is solved by matching symbol-marked metal orbs to the Seven Heroine statue bearing the same symbol -- NOT by lighting torches in a set order. Removed the invented detail 'per Rotana's research.'

Kema Zoos hint corrected: 'The Silent Swordswomen' is solved by following each swordswoman statue in the direction its sword points. The draft's 'deliver the eighth heroine's missing weapons to her statue' was wrong -- that describes the separate 'The Eighth Heroine' quest, whose statue is in the Gerudo Highlands, not this region. Location refined to 'northwest of the West Gerudo Ruins.'
Kay Noh hint corrected: 'Power of Electricity' uses Magnesis to place metal cubes in water to conduct current to switches -- not 'guide an electric orb through metal rails.'
Daqo Chisay hint refined: 'The Whole Picture' uses Magnesis to bridge gaps in the floor wires with metal objects (the wires are fixed, the metal objects are moved).

The Mystery Polluter corrected: the giver is Dalia (her garden is being polluted), not Lorah/Furosa, and there is no spa night-stakeout. You follow a trail of Hydromelon rinds to Calyban on the wall by the water. Removed the spa/night details.

Thunder Helm corrected: it requires only FOUR Gerudo Town favors (The Search for Barta, Tools of the Trade, Medicinal Molduga, The Mystery Polluter), not five. The Secret Club's Secret is optional and is NOT a prerequisite; removed 'counts toward the Thunder Helm' from it and from the redundant entries.
The Search for Barta giver set to Liana (at the Gerudo Town barracks); removed the unverified 'Leena' alias and the specific 'Silver Rupee' reward claim.
Secret Club's Secret detail corrected: the password is 'GSC' followed by a diamond symbol (learned by eavesdropping in The Noble Canteen); the club sells the Desert Voe and Radiant sets.

Removed the duplicate/garbled final side-quest entry ('Tools of the Trade / Forbidden City Entry'), which conflated a shrine-region side quest with a main-quest disguise step; the vai-disguise requirement is captured in notes instead.
Dila Maag reward specified as the Barbarian Armor chest piece (the body armor) found in the shrine's chest.
Suma Sahma hint made concrete: read the Mountain Peak Log, then at 4PM cast a cold shadow (snowball or Cryonis block) on the glowing pedestal to raise the shrine.
Misae Suma location refined to the East Barrens east of the Southern Oasis, and the hint clarified (wake the sleeping Gerudo Pokki by providing the drink Noble Pursuit).

Wasteland Tower location/climb tip rewritten: the tower stands on a rock platform amid a sinking-sand basin at the desert's western edge (the draft's 'bottomless bog on Spectacle Rock at the north edge / Gerudo Highlands border' mixed in incorrect terrain and placement).
Great Fairy Tera confirmed correct (rib cage of the Gerudo Great Skeleton beside Hawa Koth); cost tiers verified.

tower.name: Wasteland Tower
tower.location: On a rock spire in the Gerudo Desert, northwest of Divine Beast Vah Naboris, northeast of Gerudo Town and southwest of the Gerudo Great Skeleton.
tower.climbTip: Climb at night to avoid daytime heat damage, or wear Gerudo/heat-resist gear; bring stamina food since the spire is tall. Watch for hazards below.

shrines.name: Daqo Chisay Shrine
shrines.location: Just outside the main gate of Gerudo Town, a few steps from the entrance plaza.
shrines.oneLine: The Whole Picture: use Magnesis to place metal barrels/cubes on the green lines so electricity flows to the switches. Quickest shrine to reach here.

shrines.name: Jee Noh Shrine
shrines.location: At the bottom of a deep gorge just northwest of the Wasteland Tower; reachable along the path from Gerudo Canyon Stable.
shrines.oneLine: On Move: shoot the rolling orb into the bowl, using Stasis on the orb or platform to line up your arrow, then repeat through each room.

shrines.name: Kay Noh Shrine
shrines.location: On a small cliff right next to Gerudo Canyon Stable, at the canyon entrance to the desert.
shrines.oneLine: Power of Electricity: move the charged ball to slots and use Magnesis to arrange metal cubes in the water so current reaches both switches.

shrines.name: Misae Suma Shrine
shrines.location: In the East Barrens, southeast of Gerudo Town behind a rocky outcrop in the open desert.
shrines.oneLine: A Blessing shrine, but first do The Perfect Drink: bring Pokki a Noble Pursuit from Gerudo Town's canteen, then activate the shrine.

shrines.name: Tho Kayu Shrine
shrines.location: At the Toruma Dunes, west of Gerudo Town.
shrines.oneLine: Buried until you light the torches scattered around it; once they're all lit the Blessing shrine rises from the sand. Free orb inside.

shrines.name: Hawa Koth Shrine
shrines.location: Beneath the Gerudo Great Skeleton at Dragon's Exile, far southwest corner of the Gerudo Desert; reach it via the Karusa Valley sandstorm.
shrines.oneLine: The Current Solution: use Magnesis on metal blocks to conduct electricity along the circuits and power the switches that open the path.

shrines.name: Dako Tah Shrine
shrines.location: At the Great Cliffs north of Kara Kara Bazaar, in ruins southeast of the Gerudo Tower, hidden inside a sandstorm.
shrines.oneLine: Eye of the Sandstorm: talk to Nobiro at Kara Kara Bazaar, then cross the Great Cliffs at night (9pm-4am) when the sandstorm clears.

shrines.name: Suma Sahma Shrine
shrines.location: Atop Mount Granajh in the Gerudo Highlands, southeast of the Wasteland Tower.
shrines.oneLine: Read the Mountain Peak Log near the cabin, then at 4pm cast a cold shadow (snowball or Cryonis block) onto the glowing pedestal.
shrines.shrineQuest: The Secret of the Snowy Peaks

shrines.name: Raqa Zunzo Shrine
shrines.location: At the Sand-Seal Rally racecourse, southeast of Gerudo Town.
shrines.oneLine: Undefeated Champ: talk to Shabonne, then win the sand-seal race through all 8 arches, beating Tali's record of 1:30, to raise the shrine.

shrines.name: Dila Maag Shrine
shrines.location: At the center of the South Lomei Labyrinth, southeast end of the Gerudo Desert near Mount Granajh.
shrines.oneLine: Navigate the South Lomei Labyrinth to its heart. The shrine itself is a Blessing with no inner trial.

shrines.name: Kema Zoos Shrine
shrines.location: In the West Barrens (northwest desert), west of the Gerudo Tower and Karusa Valley.
shrines.oneLine: Silent Swordswomen: talk to Laine at Gerudo Town's NW gate, then follow each statue's sword-pointing direction to the next until the shrine.

shrines.name: Korsh O'hu Shrine
shrines.location: At the Seven Heroines statues, in ruins east of Gerudo Town and southwest of the Wasteland Tower.
shrines.oneLine: Seven Heroines: talk to Rotana in Gerudo Town, then place each symbol-marked orb in the pedestal of the statue bearing the same symbol.

sideQuests.name: The Eighth Heroine
sideQuests.giver: Bozai (outside Gerudo Town's gate, near Daqo Chisay Shrine)
sideQuests.oneLine: Wearing the Gerudo outfit, photograph the Statue of the Eighth Heroine in the snowy Gerudo Highlands for Bozai. He lends Snow Boots to help.

sideQuests.name: The Forgotten Sword
sideQuests.giver: Bozai (Gerudo Town, follow-up after The Eighth Heroine)
sideQuests.oneLine: After The Eighth Heroine, find the heroine's missing sword on the snowfield near her statue and report back to Bozai.

sideQuests.name: Tools of the Trade
sideQuests.giver: Isha (outside the jewelry shop in Gerudo Town)
sideQuests.reward: A gem accessory of your choice (Ruby Circlet, Sapphire Circlet, or Topaz Earrings)
sideQuests.oneLine: Bring Isha 10 flint so she can reopen her jewelry shop; pick one gemstone accessory as thanks.

sideQuests.name: Medicinal Molduga
sideQuests.oneLine: Hunt a Molduga in the desert and deliver its Molduga Guts to Malena to help cure her ailing partner.

sideQuests.name: The Search for Barta
sideQuests.giver: Liana (Gerudo Town training area, near the throne room)
sideQuests.oneLine: A vai who escaped the Yiga Hideout went missing; find Barta resting at the Gerudo Great Skeleton and report back to Liana.

sideQuests.name: The Secret Club's Secret
sideQuests.reward: Access to the hidden Gerudo Secret Club shop (Desert Voe and Radiant gear)
sideQuests.oneLine: Eavesdrop in the canteen to learn the club's password, then give it to Greta to enter the secret shop beneath the canteen.

greatFairy.name: Great Fairy Tera
greatFairy.location: Inside the rib cage of the Gerudo Great Skeleton at Dragon's Exile, far southwest corner of the Gerudo Desert, right next to Hawa Koth Shrine.
greatFairy.unlockCost: Up to 10,000 Rupees (the price scales with how many fairies you've already freed; Tera demands 10,000 if she is your fourth and last).

notes: Roster verified at 12 shrines for the Wasteland Tower region against Game8, Zelda Dungeon, Gamer Guides and Zelda Wiki (Fandom): Daqo Chisay, Jee Noh, Kay Noh, Misae Suma, Tho Kayu, Hawa Koth, Dako Tah, Suma Sahma, Raqa Zunzo, Dila Maag, Kema Zoos, Korsh O'hu. Correct open-access vs quest-gated split: 5 are open-access (Daqo Chisay, Jee Noh, Kay Noh, Tho Kayu, Hawa Koth) and 7 are unlocked by shrine quests (Misae Suma via The Perfect Drink, Dako Tah, Suma Sahma, Raqa Zunzo, Dila Maag, Kema Zoos, Korsh O'hu). The draft wrongly treated Misae Suma as a free open-access Blessing; it is actually gated behind The Perfect Drink quest. The shrines of the GERUDO Tower region to the south (Joloo Nah, Sho Dantu, Keeha Yoog, Mezza Lo, Daag Chokah, Ke'nai Shakah) are a separate region and not counted here. HAZARDS: extreme desert heat by day (need heat-resist - Gerudo set, chilly/hearty food) and freezing cold at night in the highlands (need cold-resist or Snow Boots). Sandstorms disable the Sheikah Slate map and Sheikah Sensor. Molduga roam the open sand (lure/stun with bomb arrows, then strike). Yiga footsoldiers ambush desert roads, and the Yiga Clan Hideout requires stealth (the Gerudo set does not disguise you there). RECOMMENDED GEAR: Gerudo vai outfit (also required to enter Gerudo Town and to start Bozai's quests), plus Sand Boots and Snow Boots from Bozai, and heat/cold meals. Lower-confidence item: the exact in-game wording of the Gerudo Secret Club password - the game renders it with a symbol, so I avoided stating a precise spelling like 'GSC4EVA' which is an unverified fan transcription.
changes: Daqo Chisay: changed category blessing->puzzle and rewrote the hint; it is NOT a free-orb shrine but the 'The Whole Picture' Magnesis/electric-conductivity puzzle (kept the 'quickest to reach' note).

Jee Noh: corrected location (deep gorge NW of the Wasteland Tower, off the Gerudo Canyon Stable path - not 'south of Mount Nabooru near the eastern desert entrance') and replaced the invented metal-cube/magnesis hint with the real 'On Move' trial (shoot the rolling orb into the bowl using Stasis).
Kay Noh: replaced the invented 'tilt/motion-control ball maze' hint with the actual 'Power of Electricity' puzzle (charged ball + Magnesis circuits to two switches). Location was already correct.

Misae Suma: MAJOR fix - it is gated behind the shrine quest 'The Perfect Drink' (bring Pokki a Noble Pursuit), so set shrineQuest to 'The Perfect Drink' and category blessing->quest; corrected location to East Barrens SE of Gerudo Town and replaced the 'only challenge is crossing sand' hint.
Tho Kayu: corrected the reveal mechanic - the shrine rises when you LIGHT the surrounding torches, not by digging it out of the sand.

Hawa Koth: replaced the invented 'fan-driven sand conveyor + stasis on flowing sand' hint with the real 'The Current Solution' puzzle (Magnesis + electricity). Clarified it sits beneath the Gerudo Great Skeleton, reached via Karusa Valley.

Korsh O'hu: corrected quest giver to Rotana (in NW Gerudo Town) and replaced the invented 'light braziers to match elements' solution with the real one - match each symbol-marked orb to the statue bearing the same symbol; refined location (Seven Heroines ruins SW of the Wasteland Tower, dropped the off 'Daval Peak' reference).
Raqa Zunzo: added the quest giver Shabonne and clarified the race is through 8 arches; location simplified to the Sand-Seal Rally SE of Gerudo Town.
Tower: corrected/clarified the location relative to Divine Beast Vah Naboris (northwest of Naboris) and softened the unverified 'sand-blowing geysers' detail.

The Search for Barta: corrected Barta's hiding spot to the Gerudo Great Skeleton (not Karusa Valley), set the reward to 100 Rupees, and named the giver as Liana (dropped the speculative 'Captain Teake').
Medicinal Molduga: reward confirmed as a Gold Rupee (300 Rupees) from Malena.
The Secret Club's Secret: softened the password to avoid stating the unverified fan spelling 'GSC4EVA'; clarified the reward is the Gerudo Secret Club shop (Desert Voe / Radiant gear).

The Eighth Heroine / The Forgotten Sword: confirmed the draft's order and rewards are correct (Eighth Heroine first -> Sand Boots; Forgotten Sword second -> Snow Boots) and noted Bozai lends Snow Boots during the first quest.
Great Fairy Tera: confirmed correct (rib cage of the Gerudo Great Skeleton, next to Hawa Koth); clarified the unlock cost scales with fairies freed (up to 10,000 if she is the fourth).

Notes: rewrote the misleading claim about SAMURAI GAMERS reversing Dako Tah/Suma Sahma (both are genuinely quest shrines) and corrected the open-access vs quest-gated counts to 5 open / 7 quest after fixing Misae Suma.

tower.name: Ridgeland Tower
tower.location: West-central Hyrule Ridge, set in a small lake; it faces east toward Thundra Plateau, west of the Central Tower region
tower.climbTip: The base lake holds Electric Wizzrobes and Electric Lizalfos that can shock you off the wall, so clear or avoid them first; rubber armor or shock-resist food helps. Climb the inner struts to dodge obstacles and pace your stamina.

shrines.name: Maag No'rah Shrine
shrines.location: Inside a cliff cave southwest of the Maritta Exchange Ruins; there's a Bokoblin camp with a tall lookout tower nearby - face south from the tower for the cracked rocks
shrines.oneLine: "Maag No'rah's Blessing" - no trial inside; blow open the cracked rocks on the cliff with a bomb arrow or remote bomb, climb in, and claim the orb.

shrines.name: Mogg Latan Shrine
shrines.location: Atop Satori Mountain, southwest of Ridgeland Tower, south of Hyrule Ridge
shrines.oneLine: "Synced Swing" - use Magnesis (and Stasis) to swing and steady the chained metal platforms, timing your jumps across the gaps to the monk.

shrines.name: Zalta Wa Shrine
shrines.location: In the Breach of Demise, right along the path just southeast of Ridgeland Tower
shrines.oneLine: "Two Orbs to Guide You" - shoot the first orb into its slot, carry the freed orb onto the floor switch, then use the shock-launch platform to reach the altar.

shrines.name: Mijah Rokee Shrine
shrines.location: Southern part of Washa's Bluff, north of Satori Mountain past the Tamio River; Kass sits in a tree nearby
shrines.oneLine: Find Kass for "Under a Red Moon," then stand bare on the pedestal during a Blood Moon to raise it; inside is "A Modest Test of Strength" (Guardian Scout III).

shrines.name: Sheem Dagoze Shrine
shrines.location: On a cliffside in the West Hyrule Plains, northwest of Jeddo Bridge overlooking the river; Kass is nearby
shrines.oneLine: Solve "The Two Rings" - shoot one arrow through both stone rings to raise the shrine; inside, guide two Ancient Orbs down the ramps into their slots.

shrines.name: Toh Yahsa Shrine
shrines.location: On Thundra Plateau, west of Ridgeland Tower
shrines.oneLine: "Trial of Thunder" - use Stasis to knock the four colored orbs into their matching slots on the plateau to raise the shrine; inside is a short Magnesis puzzle.

shrines.name: Shae Loya Shrine
shrines.location: On a ridge in Hyrule Ridge just south of Tabantha Bridge Stable
shrines.oneLine: "Aim for the Moment" - ride the rotating platforms and shoot the eye switches with arrows at the right instant to open the gates to the monk.

shrines.name: Shira Gomar Shrine
shrines.location: Rises from the south side of Tanagar Canyon, above the canyon near Upland Lindor (DLC: The Champions' Ballad)
shrines.oneLine: "Aim for Stillness" DLC shrine - talk to Mazli by the white-smoke campfire, wait for Dinraal at night, then shoot the dragon's horn to make it rise.
shrines.shrineQuest: EX Champion Revali's Song (The Champions' Ballad DLC)

sideQuests.name: A Gift for the Great Fairy
sideQuests.reward: Access to Great Fairy Kaysa's fountain (armor upgrades)
sideQuests.oneLine: Toren hands you his 500-rupee life savings to wake the Great Fairy; carry it southwest to Kaysa's fountain on Piper Ridge and pay her to open it.

sideQuests.name: Misko, the Great Bandit
sideQuests.giver: Domidak and Prissen at Dueling Peaks Stable
sideQuests.oneLine: Started at Dueling Peaks Stable, but the clue points to ruined pillars where Rayne, Piper, and Tanagar meet in Hyrule Ridge - dig there for the stash.

notes: Base game: 7 shrines in the Ridgeland Tower region (Maag No'rah, Mogg Latan, Zalta Wa, Mijah Rokee, Sheem Dagoze, Toh Yahsa, Shae Loya), plus the DLC Shira Gomar (Champions' Ballad) for 8 total - roster membership in the draft was correct. KEY FIXES vs draft: (1) Mijah Rokee is NOT a free-orb blessing - the shrine quest \"Under a Red Moon\" (Kass) makes it rise when you stand bare during a Blood Moon, and inside it's \"A Modest Test of Strength\" combat (Guardian Scout III); recategorized to mixed and added the quest. (2) Sheem Dagoze's trial is NOT \"Moving in Parallel\" with Magnesis panels (invented) - it's unlocked by \"The Two Rings\" (shoot an arrow through two stone rings) and the interior is the two-orb ramp puzzle; recategorized to quest, location moved to NW of Jeddo Bridge in West Hyrule Plains. (3) Zalta Wa is in the Breach of Demise just SE of Ridgeland Tower, NOT at Lindor's Brow; trial name \"Two Orbs to Guide You\" was correct. (4) Toh Yahsa is on Thundra Plateau west of the tower (NOT the Mount Daphnes/Tanagar side) and is unlocked by the \"Trial of Thunder\" shrine quest; recategorized to quest. (5) Shira Gomar's trial name is \"Aim for Stillness\" and reveal is via Mazli + shooting Dinraal's horn at night - confirmed. (6) Great Fairy Kaysa's fountain sits in the Tabantha Tower region (Piper Ridge), not Ridgeland, so greatFairy stays null - correct. HAZARDS: tower lake has Electric Wizzrobes/Lizalfos (rubber set or shock-resist meals); Hebra/Tabantha edges get cold at night (warm doublet or spicy food). CONFIDENCE: high on names/categories/quests; medium on a couple of fine landmark coordinates.
changes: Mijah Rokee: changed category from 'blessing' to 'mixed' and corrected the reward - it is NOT a free orb; it's unlocked by shrine quest 'Under a Red Moon' (Kass) and contains 'A Modest Test of Strength' combat (Guardian Scout III). Added shrineQuest 'Under a Red Moon' and rewrote the hint.

Sheem Dagoze: removed invented trial 'Moving in Parallel' / Magnesis-panel solution. Correct trial is the two-Ancient-Orb ramp puzzle, unlocked by the shrine quest 'The Two Rings' (shoot one arrow through two stone rings). Changed category from 'puzzle' to 'quest', added shrineQuest, fixed location to NW of Jeddo Bridge (West Hyrule Plains), rewrote hint.

Zalta Wa: fixed location - it is in the Breach of Demise just southeast of Ridgeland Tower, NOT in a cave at Lindor's Brow between Ridgeland and Hebra. Tightened the hint to the actual solution (orb-into-slot + floor switch + shock-launch platform). Trial name was already correct.

Toh Yahsa: fixed location from 'Mount Daphnes / Tanagar Canyon side' to Thundra Plateau west of Ridgeland Tower. Changed category from 'puzzle' to 'quest' and added shrineQuest 'Trial of Thunder' (the four-colored-orb outdoor puzzle that raises the shrine); clarified the interior is a short Magnesis puzzle ('Buried Secrets').
Shae Loya: refined location to a ridge just south of Tabantha Bridge Stable. Trial name 'Aim for the Moment' and puzzle category confirmed correct.
Mogg Latan: confirmed 'Synced Swing' puzzle on Satori Mountain; noted it uses Stasis as well as Magnesis. No change to category.
Shira Gomar: added the trial name 'Aim for Stillness' and corrected the reveal detail (talk to Mazli, wait for Dinraal at night, shoot the dragon's horn). DLC/quest classification confirmed correct.
Maag No'rah: confirmed 'blessing' with bomb-arrow cave entry SW of Maritta Exchange Ruins; tidied hint. No category change.
Great Fairy: confirmed greatFairy stays null - Kaysa's fountain (Piper Ridge) is in the Tabantha Tower region, not Ridgeland. Draft was correct.
Side quest 'A Gift for the Great Fairy': corrected the reward wording - Link does not keep the 500 rupees; he pays Toren's 500-rupee savings to Kaysa to unlock her fountain.
Tower climbTip: removed the unverifiable 'ride updrafts from nearby fires' detail and kept the verified electric-enemy hazard guidance.

tower.name: Tabantha Tower
tower.location: Atop Nero Hill in the Tabantha Frontier, northwest Hyrule, between Rito Village and the Hebra Mountains. Reach it up the snowy slope from the Tabantha Great Bridge.
tower.climbTip: Malice covers the base and lower walls. Burn the Malice eyeball spikes near Nero Hill, then climb the clean stone above. Bring warm gear or spicy food for the cold.

shrines.name: Akh Va'quot Shrine
shrines.location: In Rito Village itself, on a separate stone spire reached by the wooden bridge near the top of the village's spiraling walkway.
shrines.oneLine: Trial of the Windmills: raise the metal sail-fans into the wind so the propellers spin and move platforms; ride them up to reach the monk.
