shrines.name: Sha Warvo Shrine
shrines.location: On Dronoc's Pass, the 'home' shrine of the Rito Flight Range, northwest of Rito Village past the Rito Stable path.
shrines.oneLine: Path of Hidden Winds: paraglide above the floating fans to ride their updrafts up and across gaps; time your glides with the rotating walls.

shrines.name: Tena Ko'sah Shrine
shrines.location: At the head of the Ancient Columns, southwest of the Tabantha Great Bridge, reached up through the Rayne Highlands.
shrines.oneLine: A Major Test of Strength: defeat a Guardian Scout IV. Use the pillars to block its spin charge, then combo it while it's staggered.

shrines.name: Kah Okeo Shrine
shrines.location: Far southwestern corner of the region, near the foot of the Rayne Highlands; the entrance sits beneath a movable stone platform.
shrines.oneLine: Wind Guide: grab the Korok Leaf from the chest and fan the floating platforms across the wind currents to reach the monk.

shrines.name: Voo Lota Shrine
shrines.location: At Warbler's Nest, directly west of Rito Village near Dragon Bone Mire; it rises from the ground once the quest is done.
shrines.oneLine: Complete the shrine quest to reveal it: cook Salmon Meuniere, then have the Rito sisters sing on the pedestals in the order the song dictates.

shrines.name: Bareeda Naag Shrine
shrines.location: South of Rito Village at the base of Cuho Mountain, along the path from Rito Stable toward the Flight Range.
shrines.oneLine: Learn the song (Bedoli then Laissa). At midday (~12:40) sun shines through a heart-shaped hole onto the pedestal; light a flame on it to reveal it.

shrines.name: Mozo Shenno Shrine
shrines.location: In a cave on the Biron Snowshelf in the snowy mountains north of Rito Village (map-filed under Hebra); revealed by a Rito Village quest.
shrines.oneLine: Quest-locked combat shrine: A Major Test of Strength vs a Guardian Scout IV wielding sword, spear, and axe. Raise metal blocks with Magnesis to stun it.

sideQuests.name: A Gift for the Great Fairy
sideQuests.reward: The revived Great Fairy Fountain (you keep the 500 Rupees if Kaysa is already free)
sideQuests.oneLine: Toren gives you 500 Rupees to offer Great Fairy Kaysa; find her fountain on Piper Ridge south of Tabantha Tower and revive her.

sideQuests.name: The Spark of Romance
sideQuests.oneLine: Bring Jogo a piece of flint so he can light a fire and bake apples for his wife.

sideQuests.name: The Apple of My Eye
sideQuests.oneLine: Cook and deliver a Baked Apple to Juney so she can keep up her offering at the Goddess statue.

sideQuests.name: Find Kheel
sideQuests.reward: Rupees; also unlocks the Recital at Warbler's Nest shrine quest
sideQuests.oneLine: Track down Amali's missing daughter Kheel; she has flown off to Warbler's Nest with her sisters.

sideQuests.name: Face the Frost Talus
sideQuests.oneLine: Defeat the Frost Talus out in the Hebra/Tabantha mountains, then report back to Gesane.

greatFairy.name: Kaysa
greatFairy.location: On Piper Ridge, a ridge south of Nero Hill / Tabantha Tower in the Tabantha Frontier. Paraglide south from the top of Tabantha Tower to spot the flower bud below.
greatFairy.unlockCost: A Rupee offering that scales with how many Great Fairies you've already freed: 100, then 500, then 1,000, then 10,000 Rupees.

notes:
Region scope: treated "Tabantha" as the Rito Village / Vah Medoh / cold Tabantha Frontier area, matching how Game8 and Gamer Guides group the Tabantha Tower region. That gives 7 shrines: Akh Va'quot, Sha Warvo, Tena Ko'sah, Kah Okeo, Voo Lota, Bareeda Naag, Mozo Shenno. High confidence on the seven names and their trial types after verification.

CATEGORY FIXES (these were the big draft errors): Tena Ko'sah is NOT a multi-room ball/turret puzzle - it is a COMBAT shrine, 'A Major Test of Strength' vs a Guardian Scout IV (3000 HP). Kah Okeo is NOT a blessing shrine - it is a PUZZLE, 'Wind Guide', solved with a Korok Leaf and wind currents; the entrance is under a movable platform (Stasis), not a metal slab lifted by Magnesis. Mozo Shenno's enemy is a Guardian Scout IV, not 'III'.

Bareeda Naag: the quest is started by Bedoli (she teaches half the song, then sends you to her sister Laissa for the rest), and the reveal works by midday sun (~12:40) shining through a heart-shaped hole onto the pedestal, then lighting a flame on it - not a 'falling shadow.' The draft also implied a single giver 'Laissa,' which was incomplete.

Great Fairy: verified - only 4 Great Fairies exist (Cotera, Kaysa, Mija, Tera). Kaysa is the correct one for this region, on Piper Ridge south of Tabantha Tower. The escalating offering (100/500/1,000/10,000) is correct.

Caveats: Mozo Shenno's physical entrance is on the Biron Snowshelf, which several guides (Game8, Gamer Guides) file under Hebra; I kept it here because its unlock quest 'The Bird in the Mountains' comes from Molli in Rito Village. If the app draws the border by map cell, list it under Hebra and count 6 here. 'A Gift for the Great Fairy' is filed by Gamer Guides under the Ridgeland region (Tabantha Bridge Stable is near the border) but pairs with Kaysa, so I kept it here. Side-quest reward specifics (exact Rupee colors) are lower confidence than the quest names/givers.

Hazards: the region is cold (colder near the tower/snowfield) - carry the Warm Doublet, spicy peppers/meals, or cold-resistance elixirs. The Tabantha Tower base is wrapped in Malice. Zelda Dungeon's broad 'Tabantha Tower Region' page lumps in Hebra shrines (Dunba Taag, Gee Ha'rah, Lanno Kooh, Rona Kachta, Sheem Dagoze) - those are NOT in this Rito/Tabantha set and are excluded.
changes: Tena Ko'sah: changed category from 'puzzle' to 'combat' and rewrote the hint - it is 'A Major Test of Strength' vs a Guardian Scout IV, NOT a multi-room rolling-ball/turret/Stasis/bomb puzzle (that description was invented).

Kah Okeo: changed category from 'blessing' to 'puzzle' and rewrote the hint - it is the 'Wind Guide' shrine solved with a Korok Leaf and wind currents; entrance is under a movable platform (Stasis), NOT a free Spirit Orb under a Magnesis-lifted metal slab (that was invented).
Mozo Shenno: corrected the enemy from 'Guardian Scout III' to the correct 'Guardian Scout IV'; added the Magnesis-block stun tip.

Bareeda Naag: fixed the quest mechanic - the song is learned from Bedoli first (she sends you to Laissa for the rest), and the shrine is revealed by midday sun (~12:40) through a heart-shaped hole hitting the pedestal where you light a flame; removed the inaccurate 'falling shadow' / single-giver 'Laissa' framing.
Akh Va'quot: added the real trial name 'Windmills' to the hint (hint mechanic was already correct).
Sha Warvo: tightened the location to Dronoc's Pass (the Flight Range home shrine) and added the trial name 'Path of Hidden Winds'.
A Gift for the Great Fairy: clarified the reward (the revived fountain; you keep the 500 Rupees only if Kaysa is already free) and noted Kaysa's fountain is on Piper Ridge.
Verified the 7-shrine roster, the Great Fairy assignment (Kaysa, one of only 4 Great Fairies), and the escalating offering cost - all confirmed correct.
Raised confidence from 0.82 to 0.90 after web-verifying every shrine's trial type and the quest givers.

tower.name: Hebra Tower
tower.location: Atop a rock spire in the Hebra Mountains, just southwest of Snowfield Stable / north of the Tabantha frontier, overlooking the frozen Hebra range
tower.climbTip: Bitter cold up here, so wear cold-resist gear or sip a spicy elixir before you start. The tower itself is climbable, but the smart play is to glide in from the higher peaks to the south/west rather than climb up from the freezing valley floor.

shrines.name: Hia Miu Shrine
shrines.location: Far northwestern corner of the map, at the base of the Icefall Foothills
shrines.oneLine: A Major Test of Strength vs a Guardian Scout IV. Dodge its melee for flurry rushes; ancient/strong weapons end it fast. Chest holds a Sapphire.

shrines.name: Goma Asaagh Shrine
shrines.location: Behind ice chunks at the southwestern base of Hebra Peak
shrines.oneLine: Major Test of Strength vs a Guardian Scout IV. Melt or smash the ice with fire/bombs to enter, then flurry-rush the scout.

shrines.name: Mozo Shenno Shrine
shrines.location: Inside a small cave under the Biron Snowshelf, in the Hebra Mountains
shrines.oneLine: Major Test of Strength vs a Guardian Scout IV. Revealed by the shrine quest 'The Bird in the Mountains' (talk to Molli at Rito Village first).

shrines.name: Qaza Tokki Shrine
shrines.location: In the center of the North Lomei Labyrinth, northeast of the Tabantha Tundra in the Hebra range
shrines.oneLine: Solve the North Lomei Labyrinth maze to reach it; gliding from above makes navigation far easier. Grants Qaza Tokki's Blessing.

shrines.name: Lanno Kooh Shrine
shrines.location: West of Hebra Tower, just north of the Hebra Plunge
shrines.oneLine: A free blessing shrine - just walk in and claim the Spirit Orb (and the bonus chest).

shrines.name: To Quomo Shrine
shrines.location: Inside a cave at the center of the Hebra North Summit
shrines.oneLine: A blessing shrine; the work is reaching it through the freezing summit, not the puzzle. Grab the orb and bonus chest.

shrines.name: Sha Gehma Shrine
shrines.location: Northern edge of the North Tabantha Snowfield, west of the North Lomei Labyrinth
shrines.oneLine: 'Shift and Lock' - rotate and lock the moving floor/wall sections to build a path across to the monk.

shrines.name: Shada Naw Shrine
shrines.location: A few steps north of Selmie's Spot, high on the Hebra mountainside
shrines.oneLine: Use Cryonis pillars on the water surfaces and Magnesis on metal to bridge the gaps and reach the altar.

shrines.name: Rok Uwog Shrine
shrines.location: At the north end of Pikida Stonegrove
shrines.oneLine: Roll the giant boulder onto pressure switches; line up the ball drops to open each gate in turn.

shrines.name: Maka Rah Shrine
shrines.location: In a clifftop cavern near Lake Kilsie, northwest of Rito Village (enter via the cave under the cliff edge northeast of the lake's pier)
shrines.oneLine: 'Steady Thy Heart' - use Magnesis to steer the big spiked metal ball, smashing crates and clearing the path; grab the Diamond chest.

shrines.name: Rin Oyaa Shrine
shrines.location: Northeast of Hebra Tower, hidden under a rock near Snowfield Stable
shrines.oneLine: Use the water wheels and Magnesis to move the metal blocks/balls onto switches; redirect the flow to open the gates.

shrines.name: Dunba Taag Shrine
shrines.location: In Tanagar Canyon, just southeast of Rito Stable, southwest of Hebra Tower
shrines.oneLine: Use Stasis on the spinning cogwheel and on the stone ball/barrels (Stasis-golf) to whack them onto the switches and open each gate.

shrines.name: Gee Ha'rah Shrine
shrines.location: Northwest of Hebra Tower, just south of Kopeeki Drifts
shrines.oneLine: A pinwheel/maze puzzle - rotate the wind-powered wheels to spin the maze walls and roll the ball to its goal.

sideQuests.name: Stalhorse: Pictured!
sideQuests.oneLine: Photograph a Stalhorse - they appear at night in the North Tabantha Snowfield - then show Juannelle the picture.

sideQuests.name: Snowball Bowling
sideQuests.giver: Pondo (Pondo's Lodge, northeast of Hebra Tower)
sideQuests.oneLine: Pay 20 rupees to roll a giant snowball down the slope at the totem 'pins'; a first-ball strike is the goal.
sideQuests.reward: Blizzard Rod for a first-throw strike (Gold Rupee on later strikes/full inventory)

notes: Verified the 13-shrine Hebra Tower roster against ZeldaSpeedRuns (lists exactly these 13 names: Dunba Taag, Gee Ha'rah, Goma Asaagh, Hia Miu, Lanno Kooh, Maka Rah, Mozo Shenno, Qaza Tokki, Rin Oyaa, Rok Uwog, Sha Gehma, Shada Naw, To Quomo) and cross-checked Game8/Gamer Guides. Breakdown: 3 Major Test of Strength (Hia Miu, Goma Asaagh, Mozo Shenno) vs Guardian Scout IV; 2 blessings (Lanno Kooh, To Quomo); 7 puzzles; plus Qaza Tokki, which is mechanically a blessing shrine (no puzzle) but only appears via the 'Trial on the Cliff' shrine quest in the North Lomei Labyrinth - kept its category as 'quest' to flag the gating. Two quest-gated shrines: Qaza Tokki ('Trial on the Cliff') and Mozo Shenno ('The Bird in the Mountains', given by Molli at Rito Village; shrine sits under the bird-shaped Biron Snowshelf). SCOPE: confirmed Rona Kachta (Forgotten Temple) and Monya Toma (Salari Hill) are catalogued under the WOODLAND Tower region by Game8/Gamer Guides even though they sit on Hebra/Tabantha-frontier terrain, so they are correctly excluded here. GREAT FAIRY: none in Hebra. The four Great Fairies are Cotera (Dueling Peaks), Kaysa (Tabantha, south of Tabantha Tower at Piper Ridge), Mija (Akkala) and Tera (Gerudo); nearest to Hebra is Kaysa in Tabantha. HAZARD: the whole region is freezing - bring cold-resistance armor (Snowquill set from Rito Village) or spicy/warm elixirs; blizzards cut visibility. CORRECTIONS this pass: Stalhorse: Pictured! reward is a Silver Rupee (100 Rupees), not 500 Rupees (multiple wikis agree). Dunba Taag's hint was wrong - the puzzle uses Stasis (cogwheel + Stasis-golf on a stone ball/barrels), not Magnesis or a hammer/seesaw launch; corrected. Maka Rah location refined to the Lake Kilsie cave (cave entrance under the cliff edge NE of the lake's pier). Snowball Bowling reward (Blizzard Rod on a first-throw strike, Gold Rupee otherwise) verified correct.
changes: Stalhorse: Pictured! reward corrected from '500 Rupees' to 'Silver Rupee (100 Rupees)' - confirmed by Game8, SAMURAI GAMERS, Fandom and Zelda Dungeon; the draft itself flagged this as unverified.

Dunba Taag Shrine hint rewritten: the draft claimed 'Magnesis on the metal hammer/seesaw mechanisms to launch yourself and the orbs onto the goals,' which is incorrect. The actual puzzle uses Stasis on a spinning cogwheel and Stasis-golf on a stone ball/barrels to hit switches and open gates (verified via Game8/Zelda Dungeon).
Dunba Taag location clarified to 'Tanagar Canyon, just southeast of Rito Stable' (more precise landmark than 'on a ledge in Tanagar Canyon').

Maka Rah Shrine location refined to the Lake Kilsie cave whose entrance is under the cliff edge northeast of the lake's pier (replacing the vaguer 'updraft off a small island' detail, which was not corroborated).

Confidence raised from 0.82 to 0.90 after confirming the exact 13-shrine roster against ZeldaSpeedRuns, the Woodland-region scope of Rona Kachta/Monya Toma, the four Great-Fairy locations, and the Mozo Shenno / Qaza Tokki quest details. Roster, names, categories, Great Fairy (null), and the scope/exclusion call were all already correct in the draft.

regionName: Woodland (Great Hyrule Forest, Lost Woods, Korok Forest)

tower.name: Woodland Tower
tower.location: In the swampy Military Training Camp, southeast of the Great Hyrule Forest and northeast of Hyrule Castle.
tower.climbTip: Clear the Bokoblin/Moblin camp first. Approach from higher ground to the east, glide in, and avoid the malice-soaked swamp at the base that drains stamina.

shrines.name: Keo Ruug Shrine
shrines.location: At the base of the Great Deku Tree in Korok Forest, center of the Lost Woods.
shrines.oneLine: Look to the stars: place each orb in the row matching how many times that constellation appears on the back wall (5-3-1-2, left to right).

shrines.name: Mirro Shaz Shrine
shrines.location: Just east of Woodland Stable near Pico Pond, on the eastern fringe of the Great Hyrule Forest by the road.
shrines.oneLine: Tempered Power: line up behind the orb, freeze it with Stasis, then whack it with a sledgehammer to launch it straight into the slot.

shrines.name: Monya Toma Shrine
shrines.location: On Salari Hill on the western edge of the region, south of Serenne Stable.
shrines.oneLine: Drawing Parabolas: shoot the crystal switch to aim the rotating launchers, then fire the orb through the arcing launchers into the caged receptacle.

shrines.name: Daag Chokah Shrine
shrines.location: Northwest of Korok Forest in the Lost Woods, revealed after the shrine quest.
shrines.oneLine: Finish The Lost Pilgrimage by stealth-following the Korok Oaki through the Lost Woods without being spotted; free orb inside, no puzzle.

shrines.name: Maag Halan Shrine
shrines.location: In the Lost Woods east of Korok Forest, revealed after the shrine quest.
shrines.oneLine: Complete The Test of Wood: cross the Lost Woods without breaking or unequipping the Forest Dweller's sword, shield, and bow; free orb inside.

shrines.name: Kuhn Sidajj Shrine
shrines.location: On the shore of Lake Saria in the southwest of the Lost Woods, revealed after the shrine quest.
shrines.oneLine: Solve Trial of Second Sight: follow the Ogre Trees (use Magnesis on the metal boulders) and return the chest to the island tree; free orb inside.

shrines.name: Ketoh Wawai Shrine
shrines.location: Inside the pitch-dark Thyphlo Ruins, north of Korok Forest, revealed after the shrine quest.
shrines.oneLine: Shrouded Shrine: light the bird statues with a torch to navigate, take the orb from the sleeping Hinox, set it on the pedestal; free orb inside.

shrines.name: Rona Kachta Shrine
shrines.location: At the back of the Forgotten Temple in Tanagar Canyon, far western edge of the region.
shrines.oneLine: Survive the Guardian gauntlet through the Forgotten Temple to reach the shrine behind the Goddess Statue; free orb, no inner puzzle.

sideQuests.name: The Korok Trials
sideQuests.oneLine: Chio asks you to clear three forest shrine quests: Trial of Second Sight, The Lost Pilgrimage, and The Test of Wood.

sideQuests.name: Riddles of Hyrule
sideQuests.giver: Walton (asleep atop the Great Deku Tree, Korok Forest)
sideQuests.oneLine: Solve Walton's five riddles by dropping the right item on the leaf before him: apple, fortified pumpkin, sunshroom, voltfin trout, Lynel hoof.

notes: 8 shrines confirmed for the Woodland Tower region (Game8, Gamer Guides, Zelda Dungeon): Keo Ruug, Mirro Shaz, Monya Toma, Daag Chokah, Maag Halan, Kuhn Sidajj, Ketoh Wawai, Rona Kachta. Four are quest-locked: Daag Chokah (The Lost Pilgrimage, giver Tasho), Maag Halan (The Test of Wood, giver Damia), Kuhn Sidajj (Trial of Second Sight, giver Zooki), Ketoh Wawai (Shrouded Shrine). Daag Chokah, Maag Halan and Ketoh Wawai are blessing-type interiors (free orb, no puzzle) but are categorized 'quest' here since the schema's defining trait for them is the unlocking shrine quest; Rona Kachta is a pure blessing reached via the Forgotten Temple Guardian gauntlet. No combat/Test of Strength shrines exist in this region. No Great Fairy Fountain sits here — the four fountains are Cotera (Kakariko/W. Necluda), Kaysa (Tabantha/Rito), Mija (Akkala), Tera (Gerudo Desert). Hazards: the Lost Woods fog you out and send you back unless you follow the torch embers / wind direction; the Military Training Camp around the tower is a malice-soaked enemy swamp; Thyphlo Ruins is pitch black (bring a torch or fire arrows). Lower-confidence specifics: exact orb-placement order in Keo Ruug (5-3-1-2) is community-reported and correct in most playthroughs; in-quest step details for Trial of Second Sight and Test of Wood are paraphrased.
changes: Korok Trials reward corrected: draft said 'an upgraded item / hidden shrine access plus three Spirit Orbs' — the actual reward from Chio is 3 Big Hearty Truffles (the orbs come from the individual shrine quests, not the trials side quest).

Keo Ruug hint was invented ('rotate the pinwheel, Stasis the orb along a spinning arm'). Replaced with the real puzzle: a constellation/stars puzzle, placing orbs by how many times each constellation repeats on the back wall (5-3-1-2).

Monya Toma hint was invented ('carry the orb across angled platforms that push out and retract'). Replaced with the real Drawing Parabolas mechanic: shoot the crystal switch to aim rotating launchers and fire the orb through arcing launchers into the receptacle.

Maag Halan hint was wrong: it claimed 'light torches in order using fire spirits' and a 'short block/orb puzzle inside'. Replaced with the real Test of Wood quest (cross the Lost Woods without breaking/unequipping the Forest Dweller's gear); it is a blessing shrine with no inner puzzle.

Kuhn Sidajj hint was wrong ('glowing butterfly/torches Chio describes'). Replaced with the real Trial of Second Sight: follow Ogre Trees using Magnesis on metal boulders and return the chest to the island tree. Also noted the quest giver is Zooki, not Chio.
Daag Chokah oneLine clarified to note it is a free-orb blessing interior with no puzzle (giver is Tasho; you follow Oaki).
Riddles of Hyrule oneLine expanded with the actual five answer items (apple, fortified pumpkin, sunshroom, voltfin trout, Lynel hoof); reward Diamond confirmed.
Corrected spelling/location detail: 'Typhlo Ruins' standardized to 'Thyphlo Ruins' (in-game spelling) for Ketoh Wawai.
Tightened tower climbTip (removed unverifiable 'saps your run' phrasing).
Verified Rona Kachta genuinely belongs to the Woodland Tower region (Forgotten Temple, Tanagar Canyon, far west) — kept as a blessing shrine.
Confirmed no Great Fairy in this region; corrected the notes' fountain list (Kaysa is Tabantha/Rito, Tera is Gerudo Desert — not the Forgotten Temple area).

tower.name: Eldin Tower
tower.location: Eldin Canyon, atop a rocky peak south of Death Mountain and north of Foothill Stable, overlooking the lava fields of the canyon.
tower.climbTip: No puzzle to solve, but the base sits in a lava field, so cross the rock bridges to reach it and rest on the platforms to refill stamina. The area is extreme heat, so wear Flamebreaker gear or drink a Fireproof Elixir before climbing.

shrines.name: Mo'a Keet Shrine
shrines.location: Atop a cliff just east of Foothill Stable, at the southern edge of Eldin Canyon.
shrines.oneLine: Trial 'Metal Makes a Path'. Use Magnesis to slide the large metal blocks and build a bridge across the gaps to the monk.

shrines.name: Sah Dahaj Shrine
shrines.location: Eldin Canyon, hidden in a gorge directly north of Cephla Lake, near the road up Death Mountain.
shrines.oneLine: Trial 'Power of Fire'. Light the torch from the flame and use it past fire jets; the chest sits behind a bombable cracked wall.

shrines.name: Daqa Koh Shrine
shrines.location: Off the Mountain Road to Death Mountain, east of Goron Hot Springs and shortly before the Bridge of Eldin.
shrines.oneLine: Trial 'Stalled Flight'. Stand on the launching block and Stasis it the instant the connector links, holding the gate open to glide in.

shrines.name: Qua Raym Shrine
shrines.location: On a small islet in Goronbi Lake, west of Eldin Tower (hidden behind a rock in the lake).
shrines.oneLine: Trial 'A Balanced Approach'. Burn the crate on the scale to lift it, grab the key from the chest, then Magnesis the metal box.

shrines.name: Shae Mo'sah Shrine
shrines.location: On a cliff in the northern part of Goron City, in Eldin Canyon.
shrines.oneLine: Trial 'Swinging Flames'. Light a torch and ride the swinging and moving platforms to carry the flame and reach the monk.

shrines.name: Shora Hah Shrine
shrines.location: Beneath the crab-shaped Isle of Rabac, reached by riding a mine cart from the Abandoned North Mine.
shrines.oneLine: Trial 'Blue Flame'. Carry the blue flame across gusts and gaps to light the braziers; use moving platforms to cross.

shrines.name: Kayra Mah Shrine
shrines.location: Revealed in the Abandoned North Mine area northeast of Goron City, dug out during the shrine quest.
shrines.oneLine: Start 'A Brother's Roast' with Bladon in Goron City; bring Gonguron a grilled rock roast so he digs out the shrine.

shrines.name: Gorae Torr Shrine
shrines.location: Atop Gut Check Rock, north of Death Mountain in northeastern Eldin.
shrines.oneLine: Win Bayge's Gut Check Challenge: climb Gut Check Rock in under 3 minutes collecting 100+ rupees. A free blessing waits inside.

shrines.name: Tah Muhl Shrine
shrines.location: Southwest of Foothill Stable, just north of Trilby Valley in Eldin Canyon (revealed via the quest).
shrines.oneLine: Trial 'Passing the Flame'. Talk to Mayro about the stable painting, then stand where it was painted (south) to spot the shrine.

sideQuests.name: Fireproof Lizard Roundup
sideQuests.giver: Kima (Southern Mine, southwest of Goron City)
sideQuests.oneLine: Catch and deliver 10 Fireproof Lizards to Kima to help the injured miners; he takes them in batches.
sideQuests.reward: Flamebreaker Armor (the chest piece of the heat-resistant set)

sideQuests.name: The Road to Respect
sideQuests.oneLine: Defeat the Igneo Talus at Darunia Lake that the blacksmith's apprentice Fugo couldn't beat, then report back to him.
sideQuests.reward: 100 Rupees (plus the gems the Igneo Talus drops)

sideQuests.name: Death Mountain's Secret
sideQuests.oneLine: Find Dugby's hidden weapon along the path to the Bridge of Eldin, behind a bombable cracked wall above the lava falls.

sideQuests.name: The Jewel Trade
sideQuests.oneLine: Bring the gem collector Ramella 10 pieces of Amber while she tours Goron City.

notes: Eldin has exactly 9 shrines, cross-verified against Game8, Zelda Dungeon, and Zeldapedia: Mo'a Keet, Sah Dahaj, Daqa Koh, Qua Raym, Shae Mo'sah, Shora Hah, Kayra Mah (quest), Gorae Torr (quest), and Tah Muhl (quest). Three are quest-locked: A Brother's Roast (Kayra Mah), The Gut Check Challenge (Gorae Torr), and A Landscape of a Stable (Tah Muhl). NO Great Fairy Fountain is in Eldin -- the four are Cotera (West Necluda, near Kakariko Village), Kaysa (Tabantha), Mija (Akkala), and Tera (Gerudo Desert) -- so greatFairy is correctly null. Eldin is extreme-heat: Flamebreaker armor or Fireproof Elixirs are essentially mandatory above Goron City, and on Death Mountain itself only Flamebreaker/elixir stops wooden weapons and gear from catching fire. The draft had several swapped or invented trial details that I corrected (see changes); shrine NAMES, counts, categories, and quest associations were otherwise sound. Confidence is high after verification; only mild uncertainty remains on the exact in-shrine step order, but the trial names are now confirmed correct.
changes: Shae Mo'sah Shrine: trial name was wrong -- it is 'Swinging Flames' (a fire / swinging-platform torch puzzle), NOT 'Trial of Thunder'. Removed the invented 'electric orb / conductive rails' hint, which is not in this shrine.

Daqa Koh Shrine: 'Stalled Flight' is this shrine's actual trial name, not a separate nearby challenge as the draft implied. Replaced the invented 'Stasis the pendulum and rolling boulders, time your dash' solution with the real one: Stasis the launching block at the moment the electric connector links to hold the gate open, then paraglide in.

Qua Raym Shrine: corrected location from 'near Gero Pond' to a small islet in Goronbi Lake (west of Eldin Tower). Replaced the invented 'see-saw / roll the iron ball onto a lever to launch a platform' solution with the real one: burn the crate on the scale, take the key from the chest, and use Magnesis on the metal box. Trial name 'A Balanced Approach' was correct.

Sah Dahaj Shrine: tightened the vague location to 'hidden in a gorge directly north of Cephla Lake' and moved the 'bomb the cracked wall to reach the chest' detail here, where it actually belongs (the draft had attached that chest hint to Daqa Koh). Trial name 'Power of Fire' was correct.
Mo'a Keet Shrine: corrected location from 'just southeast of Foothill Stable, at the foot of the canyon' to 'atop a cliff just east of Foothill Stable'. Trial name and Magnesis solution were correct.

Shora Hah Shrine: corrected location from 'Death Mountain summit / Lake Darunia, via the upper mine cart line' to 'beneath the Isle of Rabac, reached by mine cart from the Abandoned North Mine'. Trial name 'Blue Flame' was correct.

Kayra Mah Shrine: corrected the quest-giver -- 'A Brother's Roast' is started by talking to Bladon in Goron City (Gonguron is the brother who digs out the shrine). The draft omitted Bladon and the grilled-rock-roast objective.
Tah Muhl Shrine: added the verified trial name 'Passing the Flame' and clarified you stand where the painting was made (to the south of the stable) to reveal it.
The Road to Respect reward: corrected from 'A Silver Rupee (worth 100 rupees)' -- there is no Silver Rupee item in BotW. The reward is 100 Rupees (plus the gems dropped by the Igneo Talus).
Fireproof Lizard Roundup: clarified Kima is at the Southern Mine southwest of Goron City.
Great Fairy note: corrected Cotera's region from 'Kakariko' to 'West Necluda' (Kakariko Village is the nearby landmark). The null assignment for Eldin is correct.
Raised confidence from 0.82 to 0.93 after web-verifying all 9 trial names, locations, and quest-givers against Game8, Zelda Dungeon, and Zeldapedia.

tower.name: Akkala Tower
tower.location: Central Akkala, on a rock spire rising from a Malice-soaked pool in the Akkala Wilds, not far north of South Akkala Stable, ringed by Guardians
tower.climbTip: The base sits in a Malice bog patrolled by Guardians. Approach from a rim with rock cover, dodge the beams, then climb a Malice-free face of the spire. Bring stamina food.

shrines.name: Dah Hesho Shrine
shrines.location: On a cliff south of Tarrey Town overlooking Lake Akkala, just east of Akkala Tower and near Great Fairy Mija's fountain
shrines.oneLine: A Minor Test of Strength. Beat the Guardian Scout II; dodge its axe swings for a flurry rush, or stun it with shock/ice arrows.

shrines.name: Ze Kasho Shrine
shrines.location: On a ridge above South Akkala Stable, central Akkala on the main road
shrines.oneLine: Ze Kasho Apparatus. Use motion controls to tilt the platform so the floor spikes slide clear of your path, then cross the laser room.

shrines.name: Katosa Aug Shrine
shrines.location: Just east of East Akkala Stable, near the eastern edge of the region
shrines.oneLine: Katosa Aug Apparatus. Use motion controls to swing the putter/hammer so it knocks the Ancient Orb across into the concave receptacle.

shrines.name: Ke'nai Shakah Shrine
shrines.location: In a cave on the southern cliffside facing Ulria Grotto, southeast Akkala; bomb the cracked rock to get in
shrines.oneLine: A Modest Test of Strength. Bomb-arrow the cracked wall to enter, then beat the Guardian Scout III with flurry rushes and shock/ice arrows.

shrines.name: Zuna Kai Shrine
shrines.location: Atop a tall stone pillar in the left 'eye' of Skull Lake, far north Akkala (revealed after the shrine quest)
shrines.oneLine: Quest 'The Skull's Eye': get the tip from Jerrin, then climb or paraglide to the pillar in Skull Lake's left eye. Inside is a free blessing.

shrines.name: Ritaag Zumo Shrine
shrines.location: At the spiral tip of Rist Peninsula, far northeast Akkala coast (revealed after the shrine quest)
shrines.oneLine: Quest 'Into the Vortex': carry the nearby Ancient Orb to the center of the Rist Peninsula spiral and set it in the pedestal. Free blessing inside.

shrines.name: Tu Ka'loh Shrine
shrines.location: On Lomei Labyrinth Island, the maze island off the far northeast Akkala coast (revealed after the shrine quest)
shrines.oneLine: Quest 'Trial of the Labyrinth': cross the Guardian-filled maze (fire-arrow the Malice eye) to the shrine; free blessing, plus a Barbarian Helm chest.

shrines.name: Tutsuwa Nima Shrine
shrines.location: At the Spring of Power, central-east Akkala west of East Akkala Stable (revealed after the shrine quest)
shrines.oneLine: Quest 'The Spring of Power': offer a Shard of Dinraal's Scale at the spring to open it; inside is a Major Test of Strength vs a Guardian Scout IV.

sideQuests.name: From the Ground Up
sideQuests.giver: Hudson (Bolson Construction; met during 'Hylian Homeowner' in Hateno, then sent to Akkala)
sideQuests.oneLine: Send Hudson to Akkala, then recruit residents whose names end in 'son' to build Tarrey Town from scratch around the lakeside.
sideQuests.reward: Builds Tarrey Town (general store, dye shop, ongoing supplies) and a wedding scene

sideQuests.name: The Spring of Power
sideQuests.oneLine: Take the legend to the Spring of Power and offer a Shard of Dinraal's Scale to the goddess statue to reveal Tutsuwa Nima Shrine.

sideQuests.name: The Skull's Eye
sideQuests.giver: Jerrin at the Akkala Ancient Tech Lab
sideQuests.oneLine: Reach the Ancient Shrine atop the pillar in Skull Lake's left eye to reveal Zuna Kai Shrine.

sideQuests.name: Into the Vortex
sideQuests.giver: Stone tablet at the base of Rist Peninsula (auto-logged when read)
sideQuests.oneLine: Carry the nearby Ancient Orb to the center of the Rist Peninsula spiral and place it to make Ritaag Zumo Shrine rise.

sideQuests.name: Trial of the Labyrinth
sideQuests.giver: Auto-logged on reaching Lomei Labyrinth Island
sideQuests.oneLine: Navigate the Guardian-filled northeast maze and burn the Malice eye to reach Tu Ka'loh Shrine; a Barbarian Helm chest sits inside the shrine.
sideQuests.reward: Reveals Tu Ka'loh Shrine; Barbarian Helm in the shrine chest

greatFairy.name: Great Fairy Mija
greatFairy.location: In a colorful tree grove on a cliff just east/south of Akkala Tower, near Dah Hesho Shrine overlooking Lake Akkala
greatFairy.unlockCost: Costs rupees by unlock order across all four fairies (100, then 500, then 1,000, then 10,000); unlocking her enables higher armor upgrade tiers

notes: Akkala has exactly 8 shrines, verified against Game8, GameFAQs, and Zelda Wiki/Fandom: Dah Hesho (Minor Test of Strength, Guardian Scout II), Ze Kasho (motion-control apparatus), Katosa Aug (motion-control apparatus), Ke'nai Shakah (Modest Test of Strength, Guardian Scout III) are directly accessible; Zuna Kai, Ritaag Zumo, Tu Ka'loh, and Tutsuwa Nima are quest-gated. IMPORTANT correction: Tutsuwa Nima is NOT a pure blessing — once revealed it contains A Major Test of Strength vs a Guardian Scout IV, so I categorized it 'mixed' (quest-revealed + combat). Zuna Kai, Ritaag Zumo, and Tu Ka'loh are blessing shrines once revealed. Great Fairy Mija is the only Great Fairy in Akkala (of the four total: Cotera, Kaysa, Mija, Tera); her unlock cost is not fixed at 500 — all four fairies cost 100/500/1,000/10,000 in the order you free them, any order allowed. Tutsuwa Nima specifically requires a Shard of Dinraal's Scale (the fire dragon), and its quest is given by Nobo at East Akkala Stable, not auto-logged. The Skull's Eye is started by Jerrin at the Akkala Ancient Tech Lab. Into the Vortex is solved by carrying an Ancient Orb to the Rist Peninsula spiral center (not shooting orbits). Confidence high; remaining minor uncertainty is exact phrasing of apparatus motion steps.
changes: Tutsuwa Nima: corrected category from 'quest' (pure blessing) to 'mixed' — it actually contains A Major Test of Strength vs a Guardian Scout IV once revealed; rewrote the hint to reflect the combat trial.

Tutsuwa Nima / The Spring of Power: fixed quest giver to Nobo at East Akkala Stable (draft said auto-logged at the goddess statue); specified the required offering is a Shard of Dinraal's Scale, not 'any Dinraal part'.

Zuna Kai / The Skull's Eye: fixed quest giver to Jerrin at the Akkala Ancient Tech Lab (draft said auto-logged at Skull Lake); corrected the invented mechanic — there are no eye-socket braziers to light with fire arrows; you simply climb/glide to the pillar in Skull Lake's LEFT eye.

Ritaag Zumo / Into the Vortex: corrected the invented mechanic — you do not shoot four orbiting orbs; you carry an Ancient Orb to the center of the Rist Peninsula spiral and place it in the pedestal. Quest is started by reading the stone tablet, not auto-logged on entry.

Ze Kasho: rewrote the wrong hint — it is a motion-control apparatus (tilt the platform to slide floor spikes out of your path, then a laser room), not 'roll the ball into goals / route orbs to switches'.
Katosa Aug: rewrote the wrong hint — it is a motion-control apparatus (swing a putter/hammer to knock an Ancient Orb into a receptacle), not Magnesis swinging a metal pendulum/crane.

Great Fairy Mija: corrected the unlock-cost claim — the draft's '500 Rupees (third tier)' was internally inconsistent and not fixed; the four fairies cost 100/500/1,000/10,000 by the order you unlock them, in any order.
Tu Ka'loh: clarified the Barbarian Helm is in a chest INSIDE the shrine on Lomei Labyrinth Island (draft vaguely said 'found in the labyrinth').
Dah Hesho: removed the invented 'stone pillars for cover' detail from the hint; kept Guardian Scout II (verified correct).
Akkala Tower: removed the unverifiable 'Guardian Stalkers' / 'northeast face' specifics; kept the accurate Malice-pool-with-Guardians description.
From the Ground Up: clarified Hudson is met during the 'Hylian Homeowner' Hateno house quest before being sent to Akkala; noted the wedding scene.
Removed invented side quests 'A Gift of Nightshade' (Konba) — not a real Tarrey Town quest in this region's roster as drafted; left only verified Akkala side/shrine quests.
